title.zh: 卷十八 吳蓋陳臧列傳
title.en: Volume 18: Biographies of Wu, Gai, Chen, Zang
title.raw: 卷十八 吳蓋陳臧列傳 Volume 18: Biographies of Wu, Gai, Chen, Zang

sentences.zh: 吳漢字子顏，南陽宛人也。

translations.literal: Wu Han, courtesy name Ziyan, was a man of Wan in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han, styled Ziyan, came from Wan in Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 家貧，給事縣為亭長。

translations.literal: His family was poor; he served the county as a village head.
translations.idiomatic: Poor, he hired out to the county and became a village head.

sentences.zh: 王莽末，以賓客犯法，乃亡命至漁陽，資用乏，以販馬自業，往來燕、薊間，所至皆交結豪傑。

translations.literal: At the end of Wang Mang, because his retainers broke the law, he fled for his life to Yuyang; supplies failed and he took horse-dealing as his occupation, going back and forth between Yan and Ji; wherever he went he made ties with stalwart men.
translations.idiomatic: When his clients broke the law under Wang Mang's fall, he fled to Yuyang, sold horses between Yan and Ji for a living, and befriended bold spirits everywhere.

sentences.zh: 更始立，使使者韓鴻徇河北。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was established, he sent envoy Han Hong to tour north of the Yellow River.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi dispatched Han Hong to secure the northern commanderies.

sentences.zh: 或謂鴻曰：『吳子顏，奇士也，可與計事。

translations.literal: Someone said to Hong: 'Wu Ziyan is an extraordinary man; you may plan affairs with him.
translations.idiomatic: A man told Han Hong, 'Wu Ziyan is no ordinary talent—consult him on policy.'

sentences.zh: 』鴻召見漢，其悅之，遂承制拜為安樂令。

translations.literal: ' Hong summoned Han for audience, was very pleased with him, and thereupon acting by authority appointed him magistrate of Anle.
translations.idiomatic: Han Hong interviewed Wu Han, was delighted, and named him magistrate of Anle on his own authority.'

sentences.zh: 會王郎起，北州擾惑。

translations.literal: It happened that Wang Lang rose; the northern provinces were disturbed and confused.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Lang rebelled, the north fell into turmoil.

sentences.zh: 漢素聞光武長者，獨欲歸心。

translations.literal: Han had long heard that Guangwu was a man of integrity and alone wished to attach his heart to him.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han had long heard Guangwu praised as a true gentleman and meant to join him alone.

sentences.zh: 乃說太守彭寵曰：『漁陽、上谷突騎，天下所聞也。

translations.literal: Thereupon he persuaded Governor Peng Chong, saying: 'The shock cavalry of Yuyang and Shanggu are famed throughout the realm.
translations.idiomatic: He urged Governor Peng Chong: 'Everyone knows the shock cavalry of Yuyang and Shanggu.'

sentences.zh: 君何不合二郡精銳，附劉公擊邯鄲，此一時之功也。

translations.literal: Why do you not combine the picked troops of the two commanderies, attach yourself to Lord Liu, and strike Handan—this would be merit for one moment.
translations.idiomatic: Muster both commanderies' best men for Liu Xiu against Handan and you seize the moment.'

sentences.zh: 』寵以為然，而官屬皆欲附王郎，寵不能奪。

translations.literal: ' Chong thought it right, but his subordinates all wished to attach to Wang Lang, and Chong could not overrule them.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong agreed, yet his officers favored Wang Lang and he could not sway them.'

sentences.zh: 漢乃辭出，止外亭，念所以譎眾，未知所出。

translations.literal: Han thereupon took leave and went out, stopped at an outer post station, and thought how to deceive the host, not knowing what to do.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han withdrew to a roadside inn, racking his brains for a stratagem to turn the garrison.

sentences.zh: 望見道中有一人似儒生者，漢使人召之，為具食，問以所聞。

translations.literal: He saw in the road a man who looked like a scholar; Han sent men to summon him, prepared food for him, and asked what he had heard.
translations.idiomatic: Spotting a student-like traveler, he had him brought in, fed him, and pumped him for news.

sentences.zh: 生因言劉公所過，為郡縣所歸；

translations.literal: The student thereupon said that wherever Lord Liu passed, commanderies and counties submitted to him;
translations.idiomatic: The man said Liu Xiu's march won every county he touched;

sentences.zh: 邯鄲舉尊號者，實非劉氏。

translations.literal: the one who raised the exalted title at Handan is in truth not of the Liu clan.
translations.idiomatic: the pretender at Handan was no true Liu.'

sentences.zh: 漢大喜，即詐為光武書，移檄漁陽，使生賫以詣寵，令縣以所聞說之，漢復隨後入。

translations.literal: Han was greatly pleased, immediately forged a letter of Guangwu, moved a proclamation to Yuyang, had the student carry it to Chong, ordered the county to persuade him with what had been heard, and Han again followed in after.
translations.idiomatic: Overjoyed, Wu Han forged Guangwu's seal, drafted a proclamation for Yuyang, sent the student to Peng Chong with county corroboration, then slipped in behind.

sentences.zh: 寵甚然之。

translations.literal: Chong greatly approved it.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong accepted the plea.

sentences.zh: 於是遣漢將兵與上谷諸將並軍而南，所至擊斬王郎將帥。

translations.literal: Thereupon he dispatched Han leading troops with the generals of Shanggu to combine armies and go south; wherever they arrived they struck and beheaded Wang Lang's commanders.
translations.idiomatic: He gave Wu Han command alongside Shanggu's generals; they marched south, killing Wang Lang's officers wherever they met them.

sentences.zh: 及光武於廣阿，拜漢為偏將軍。

translations.literal: When he reached Guangwu at Guang'a, he appointed Han lieutenant general.
translations.idiomatic: At Guang'a Guangwu named Wu Han lieutenant general.

sentences.zh: 既拔邯鄲，賜號建策侯。

translations.literal: After Handan was taken, he was given the title Marquis who Establishes Strategy.
translations.idiomatic: After Handan fell he received the marquisate 'who frames strategy.'

sentences.zh: 漢為人質厚少文，造次不能以辭自達。

translations.literal: Han was by nature plain and thick, little versed in letters, and in haste could not express himself in speech.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han was blunt and inarticulate, no rhetorician in a pinch.

sentences.zh: 鄧禹及諸將我知之。

translations.literal: Deng Yu and the various generals came to know him well.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu and the other generals learned his worth.

sentences.zh: 數相薦舉，及得召見，遂見親信，賞居門下。

translations.literal: They repeatedly recommended one another; when he obtained audience, he then was seen as intimate and trusted and was kept at the gate as favorite.
translations.idiomatic: They kept recommending him until an audience won him Guangwu's trust and a place in his inner circle.

sentences.zh: 光武將發幽州兵，夜召鄧禹，問可使行者。

translations.literal: Guangwu was about to dispatch Youzhou troops; by night he summoned Deng Yu and asked whom he could send to go.
translations.idiomatic: Planning to levy Youzhou, Guangwu asked Deng Yu at night whom to send.

sentences.zh: 禹曰：『間數與吳漢言，其人勇鷙有智謀，諸將鮮能及者。

translations.literal: Yu said: 'I have several times spoken with Wu Han; that man is fierce and keen and has wisdom and stratagem; few of the generals can match him.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu said, 'I have talked often with Wu Han: he is fierce, cunning, and clever—few match him.'

sentences.zh: 』即拜漢大將軍，持節北發十郡突騎。

translations.literal: ' He immediately appointed Han grand general with staff of office and sent him north to raise the shock cavalry of ten commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu at once named him grand general with baton and sent him north to draft ten commanderies' shock cavalry.'

sentences.zh: 更始幽州牧苗曾聞之，陰勒兵，敕諸郡不肯應調。

translations.literal: Gengshi's shepherd of Youzhou Miao Zeng heard of it, secretly tightened troops, and ordered the commanderies not to respond to the levy.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi's Youzhou governor Miao Zeng mustered secretly and told the counties to ignore the call-up.

sentences.zh: 漢乃將二十騎先馳至無終。

translations.literal: Han thereupon led twenty horsemen and galloped first to Wuzhong.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han raced to Wuzhong with twenty riders.

sentences.zh: 曾以漢無備，出迎於路，漢即撝兵騎，收曾斬之，而奪其軍。

translations.literal: Zeng thought Han had no preparation and came out to welcome him on the road; Han immediately deployed armored cavalry, seized Zeng and beheaded him, and seized his army.
translations.idiomatic: Miao Zeng thought him unarmed and rode out to greet him; Wu Han sprang his horse, seized and executed Miao Zeng, and took his command.

sentences.zh: 北州震駭，城邑莫不望風弭從。

translations.literal: The northern provinces were shaken in terror; no city or town but looked to the wind and submitted.
translations.idiomatic: The north reeled; town after town submitted without a fight.

sentences.zh: 遂悉發其兵，引而南，與光武會清陽。

translations.literal: He then raised all their troops, led them south, and joined Guangwu at Qingyang.
translations.idiomatic: He led the full levy south to rendezvous with Guangwu at Qingyang.

sentences.zh: 諸將望見漢還，士馬甚盛，皆曰：『是寧肯分兵與人邪？

translations.literal: The generals saw Han return with soldiers and horses very numerous and all said: 'Would he be willing to divide troops with others?
translations.idiomatic: Seeing his huge train, the generals muttered, 'He will never share those troops.'

sentences.zh: 』及漢至莫府，上兵簿，諸將人人多請之。

translations.literal: ' When Han reached headquarters and presented the troop register, the generals each in great numbers made requests.
translations.idiomatic: Yet at headquarters Wu Han opened his rolls and every general begged for men.'

sentences.zh: 光武曰：『屬者恐不與人，今所請又何多也？

translations.literal: Guangwu said: 'A moment ago I feared you would not give men to others; now why are the requests so many?
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu laughed, 'I thought you would hoard them—why is everyone begging now?'

sentences.zh: 』諸將皆慚。

translations.literal: ' The generals were all ashamed.
translations.idiomatic: The officers looked abashed.'

sentences.zh: 初，更始遣尚書令謝躬率六將軍攻王郎，不能下。

translations.literal: At first Gengshi sent imperial secretary Xie Gong leading six generals to attack Wang Lang and could not take him.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi had sent Xie Gong with six generals against Wang Lang without success.

sentences.zh: 會光武至，共定邯鄲，而躬裨將虜掠不相承稟，光武深忌之。

translations.literal: It happened that Guangwu arrived; together they settled Handan, but Gong's lieutenant generals plundered and did not obey orders; Guangwu deeply resented it.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu joined them to take Handan, while Xie Gong's subordinates looted on their own—Guangwu watched with cold fury.

sentences.zh: 雖俱在邯鄲，遂分城而處，然每有以慰安之。

translations.literal: Though both were at Handan, they thereupon divided the city to dwell, yet whenever there was occasion he used means to comfort them.
translations.idiomatic: They quartered in separate halves of the city, yet Guangwu always soothed Xie Gong when he could.

sentences.zh: 躬勤於職事，光武常稱曰『謝尚書真吏也』，故不自疑。

translations.literal: Gong was diligent in official duties; Guangwu often praised, saying 'Imperial Secretary Xie is a true clerk,' therefore he did not suspect himself.
translations.idiomatic: Xie Gong toiled at his desk; Guangwu called him a model official, so Gong never suspected a plot.

sentences.zh: 躬既而率其兵數萬，還屯於鄴。

translations.literal: Gong afterward led his troops, several tens of thousands, returned and encamped at Ye.
translations.idiomatic: Xie Gong later marched tens of thousands back to Ye.

sentences.zh: 時光武南擊青犢，謂躬曰：『我追賊於射犬，必破之。

translations.literal: At that time Guangwu was striking south at the Green Calves; he said to Gong: 'I pursue the bandits at Shequan and am sure to break them.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu told him, 'I will crush the Green Calves at Shequan.'

sentences.zh: 尤來在山陽者，勢必當驚走。

translations.literal: Those Youlai at Shanyang in situation are sure to be alarmed and flee.
translations.idiomatic: The Youlai bands in Shanyang will panic and bolt north.'

sentences.zh: 若以君威力，擊此散虜，必成禽也。

translations.literal: If with your lordship's might and power you strike these scattered bandits, it is sure to become capture.
translations.idiomatic: Strike those stragglers with your strength and you will bag them.'

sentences.zh: 』躬曰：『善。

translations.literal: ' Gong said: 'Good.
translations.idiomatic: Xie Gong agreed.'

sentences.zh: 』及青犢破，而尤來果北走隆慮山，躬乃留大將軍劉慶、魏郡太守陳康守鄴，自率諸將軍擊之。

translations.literal: ' When the Green Calves were broken, the Youlai indeed fled north to Longlü Mountain; Gong thereupon left grand general Liu Qing and Wei commandery governor Chen Kang to guard Ye and himself led the various generals to strike them.
translations.idiomatic: After the Green Calves fell, the Youlai fled to Longlü; Xie Gong left Liu Qing and Chen Kang at Ye and gave chase.'

sentences.zh: 窮寇死戰，其鋒不可當，躬遂大敗，死者數千人。

translations.literal: Cornered bandits fight to the death; their edge could not be withstood; Gong thereupon suffered great defeat, dead numbering several thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Cornered rebels fight to the death; Xie Gong was routed with thousands slain.

sentences.zh: 光武因躬在外，乃使漢與岑彭襲其城。

translations.literal: Guangwu, because Gong was outside, thereupon sent Han with Cen Peng to raid his city.
translations.idiomatic: With Xie Gong away, Guangwu ordered Wu Han and Cen Peng to storm Ye.

sentences.zh: 漢先令辯士說陳康曰：『蓋聞上智不處危以僥幸，中智能因危以為功，下愚安於危以自亡。

translations.literal: Han first ordered a persuader to say to Chen Kang: 'I have heard that the highest wisdom does not dwell in peril to seek luck; middle wisdom can turn peril into achievement; lowest folly is content in peril and brings self-ruin.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han's envoy told Chen Kang, 'The wise flee danger, the middling exploit it, the fool hugs it to death.'

sentences.zh: 危亡之至，在人所由，不可不察。

translations.literal: The extreme of peril and destruction lies in what path men take; it cannot but be examined.
translations.idiomatic: Whether you sink or swim is the path you choose—think hard.'

sentences.zh: 今京師敗亂，四方雲擾，公所聞也。

translations.literal: Now the capital is defeated and in disorder; the four quarters swarm like clouds—this is what the duke has heard.
translations.idiomatic: The capital lies in ruins and the realm boils— you know it.'

sentences.zh: 蕭王兵強士附，河北歸命，公所見也。

translations.literal: The Prince of Xiao's troops are strong and soldiers attach; north of the Yellow River has submitted to the mandate—this is what the duke has seen.
translations.idiomatic: The Prince of Xiao's host is strong and Hebei has sworn fealty— you have seen it.'

sentences.zh: 謝躬內背蕭王，外失眾心，公所知也。

translations.literal: Xie Gong inwardly turns his back on the Prince of Xiao and outwardly has lost the hearts of the masses—this is what the duke knows.
translations.idiomatic: Xie Gong betrays the Prince of Xiao and has lost every heart— you know that too.'

sentences.zh: 公今據孤危之城，待滅亡之禍，義無所立，節無所成。

translations.literal: The duke now holds an isolated and perilous city and awaits the calamity of destruction; righteousness has nowhere to stand, integrity cannot be completed.
translations.idiomatic: You cling to a doomed town with no honor left to win.'

sentences.zh: 不若開門內軍，轉禍為禍，免下愚之敗，收中智之功，此計之至者也。

translations.literal: It is better to open the gates to the inner army, turn calamity into fortune, avoid the defeat of lowest folly, and harvest the achievement of middle wisdom—this is the utmost of plans.
translations.idiomatic: Open the gates to Han, turn peril into blessing, escape the fool's ruin, and win the wise man's reward— that is the best counsel.'

sentences.zh: 』康然之。

translations.literal: ' Kang approved it.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Kang agreed.'

sentences.zh: 於是康收劉慶及躬妻子，開門內漢等。

translations.literal: Thereupon Kang seized Liu Qing and Gong's wife and children, opened the gates, and let Han and the others in.
translations.idiomatic: He seized Liu Qing and Xie Gong's family, then admitted Wu Han.

sentences.zh: 及躬從隆慮歸鄴，不知康已反之，乃與數百騎輕入城。

translations.literal: When Gong returned from Longlü to Ye, not knowing Kang had already turned, he with several hundred cavalry lightly entered the city.
translations.idiomatic: Xie Gong rode back from Longlü unaware of the betrayal and cantered in with a few hundred horse.

sentences.zh: 漢伏兵收之，手擊殺躬，其眾悉降。

translations.literal: Han lay ambush and seized them; with his own hand he struck and killed Gong; his host all surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han's ambush took him; Wu Han killed him with his own hand and the army capitulated.

sentences.zh: 躬字子張，南陽人。

translations.literal: Gong, courtesy name Zizhang, was a man of Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Xie Gong, styled Zizhang, was from Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 初，其妻知光武不平之，常戒躬曰：『君與劉公積不相能，而信其虛談，不為人備，終受制矣。

translations.literal: At first his wife knew Guangwu was not pleased with him and often warned Gong, saying: 'You and Lord Liu have long been incompatible; yet you trust his empty talk and do not prepare against men—in the end you will be controlled.
translations.idiomatic: His wife had seen Guangwu's coldness and warned him, 'You and Liu Xiu are enemies; if you trust his smooth words and drop your guard, you will be ruined.'

sentences.zh: 』躬不納，故及於難。

translations.literal: ' Gong did not accept; therefore he met disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Xie Gong ignored her and paid with his life.'

sentences.zh: 建武二年春，漢率大司空王梁，建義大將軍朱祐，大將軍杜茂，執金吾賈復，揚化將軍堅鐔，偏將軍王霸，騎都尉劉隆、馬武、陰識，共擊檀鄉賊於鄴東漳水上，大破之。

translations.literal: In the spring of the second year of Jianwu, Han led grand minister over works Wang Liang, grand general who establishes righteousness Zhu You, grand general Du Mao, bearer of the golden mace Jia Fu, general who displays transformation Jian Tan, lieutenant general Wang Ba, chief of cavalry Liu Long, Ma Wu, and Yin Shi, jointly striking the Tanxiang bandits east of Ye on the Zhang River, and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 spring Wu Han led Wang Liang, Zhu You, Du Mao, Jia Fu, Jian Tan, Wang Ba, Liu Long, Ma Wu, and Yin Shi against the Tanxiang east of Ye on the Zhang and shattered them.

sentences.zh: 降者十餘萬人。

translations.literal: Those who surrendered were more than one hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Over a hundred thousand men yielded.

sentences.zh: 帝使使者璽書定封漢為廣平侯，食廣平、斥漳、曲周、廣年，凡四縣。

translations.literal: The emperor had an envoy with the imperial seal fix Han's fief as marquis of Guangping, with food income from Guangping, Chizhang, Quzhou, and Guangnian, four counties in all.
translations.idiomatic: The court sealed him marquis of Guangping with four counties' revenue.

sentences.zh: 復率諸將擊鄴西山賊黎伯卿等，及河內脩武，悉破諸屯聚。

translations.literal: Again he led the various generals to strike the western mountain bandits of Ye such as Li Boqing and as far as Xiuwu in Henei, and broke all the garrison settlements.
translations.idiomatic: He next cleared the western hills around Ye and the stockades at Xiuwu in Henei.

sentences.zh: 車駕親幸撫勞。

translations.literal: The imperial carriage in person visited to encourage and comfort.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor came in person to review and cheer the troops.

sentences.zh: 復遣漢進兵南陽，擊宛、涅陽、酈、穰、新野諸城、皆下之。

translations.literal: Again he dispatched Han to advance troops to Nanyang and strike Wan, Nieyang, Li, Rang, Xinye, and the various cities; all were taken.
translations.idiomatic: He sent Wu Han into Nanyang to take Wan, Nieyang, Li, Rang, and Xinye.

sentences.zh: 引兵南，與秦豐戰黃郵水上，破之。

translations.literal: He led troops south and with Qin Feng fought on the Huangyou water and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: He beat Qin Feng on the Huangyou River.

sentences.zh: 又與偏將軍馮異擊昌城五樓賊張文等，又攻銅馬、五幡於新安，皆破之。

translations.literal: Again with lieutenant general Feng Yi he struck the Changcheng Wulou bandits Zhang Wen and others, and again attacked the Bronze Horse and Five Banners at Xin'an; all were broken.
translations.idiomatic: With Feng Yi he crushed Zhang Wen's Wulou band at Changcheng and the Bronze Horse and Five Banners at Xin'an.

sentences.zh: 明年春，率建威大將軍耿弇、虎牙大將軍蓋延，擊青犢於軹西，大破降之。

translations.literal: The next spring he led grand general who establishes might Geng Yan and grand general of the tiger fang Gai Yan and struck the Green Calves west of Zhi, greatly defeated them, and made them surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Next spring he and Geng Yan and Gai Yan broke the Green Calves west of Zhi and forced their surrender.

sentences.zh: 又率驃騎大將軍杜茂、強弩將軍陳俊等，圍蘇茂於廣樂。

translations.literal: Again he led grand general of swift cavalry Du Mao, general of strong crossbows Chen Jun, and others, and besieged Su Mao at Guangle.
translations.idiomatic: He besieged Su Mao at Guangle with Du Mao and Chen Jun.

sentences.zh: 劉永將周建別招聚收集得十餘萬人，救廣樂。

translations.literal: Liu Yong's general Zhou Jian separately summoned and gathered more than one hundred thousand men and rescued Guangle.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Jian, Liu Yong's general, raised over a hundred thousand to relieve Guangle.

sentences.zh: 漢將輕騎迎與之戰，不利，墮馬傷膝，還營，建等遂連兵入城。

translations.literal: Han led light cavalry to meet and fight them; it went badly; he fell from his horse and injured his knee, returned to camp, and Jian and the others thereupon joined forces and entered the city.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han met them with light horse, was thrown, hurt his knee, and limped to camp while Zhou Jian's host poured into the town.

sentences.zh: 諸將謂漢曰：『大敵在前而公傷臥，眾心懼矣。

translations.literal: The generals said to Han: 'With a great enemy in front and the duke wounded and lying down, the hearts of the host are afraid.
translations.idiomatic: His officers said, 'The enemy is at the gates and you are bedridden— the men are afraid.'

sentences.zh: 』漢乃勃然裹創而起，椎牛饗士，令軍中曰：『賊眾雖多，皆劫掠群盜，「勝不相讓，敗不相救」，非有仗節死義者也。

translations.literal: ' Han thereupon roused himself, wrapped his wound, rose, killed an ox to feast the soldiers, and ordered in the army: 'Though the bandits are many, they are all plundering packs of robbers; "in victory they do not yield to one another, in defeat they do not rescue one another"—they are not men who hold their tally and die for righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han rose, bandaged his knee, feasted the army on beef, and shouted, 'These are only robbers who share no honor in victory nor aid in defeat—no loyal band.'

sentences.zh: 今日封侯之秋，諸君勉之！

translations.literal: Today is the season for enfeoffment as marquis; gentlemen, exert yourselves!
translations.idiomatic: Today is the day to win marquisates—fight!'

sentences.zh: 』於是軍士激怒，人倍其氣。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon the soldiers were roused to anger; each man's spirit doubled.
translations.idiomatic: The camp's fury doubled their mettle.'

sentences.zh: 旦日，建、茂出兵圍漢。

translations.literal: At dawn Jian and Mao sent troops to surround Han.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn Zhou Jian and Su Mao ringed Wu Han's camp.

sentences.zh: 漢選四部精兵黃頭吳河等，及烏桓突騎三千餘人，齊鼓而進。

translations.literal: Han selected crack troops of four divisions, Huangtou Wu He and others, and more than three thousand Wuhuan shock cavalry; with drums aligned they advanced.
translations.idiomatic: He picked four elite columns including Wu He of the yellow caps and three thousand Wuhuan horse, and rolled forward under one drumbeat.

sentences.zh: 建軍大潰，反還奔城。

translations.literal: Jian's army was greatly broken and in flight; turning back they raced to the city.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Jian's line shattered and fled for the walls.

sentences.zh: 漢長驅追擊，爭門並入，大破之，茂、建突走。

translations.literal: Han drove straight in pursuit, vying at the gates to enter together, greatly defeated them, and Mao and Jian broke through and fled.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han chased them through the gates, routed the defense, and Su Mao with Zhou Jian escaped.

sentences.zh: 漢留杜茂、陳俊等守廣樂，自將兵助蓋延圍劉永於睢陽。

translations.literal: Han left Du Mao, Chen Jun, and others to guard Guangle and himself led troops to aid Gai Yan in besieging Liu Yong at Suiyang.
translations.idiomatic: He left Du Mao and Chen Jun at Guangle and joined Gai Yan's siege of Liu Yong at Suiyang.

sentences.zh: 永既死，二城皆降。

translations.literal: When Yong had died, both cities surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: After Liu Yong died both cities came over.

sentences.zh: 明年，又率陳俊及前將軍王梁，擊破五校賊於臨平，追至東郡箕山，大破之。

translations.literal: The next year again he led Chen Jun and former general Wang Liang, broke the Five Schools band at Linping, pursued to Mount Ji in Dong commandery, and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: The following year he took Chen Jun and Wang Liang, crushed the Five Schools at Linping, and ran them to Mount Ji in Dong commandery.

sentences.zh: 北擊清河長直及平原五里賊，皆平之。

translations.literal: North he struck the chief of Qinghe Zhang Zhi and the bandits of Wuli in Pingyuan; all were pacified.
translations.idiomatic: He cleared Zhang Zhi in Qinghe and the Wuli bandits in Pingyuan.

sentences.zh: 時，鬲縣五姓共逐守長，據城而反。

translations.literal: At that time the five surnames of Ge county jointly drove out the magistrate, held the city, and rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: Five clans in Ge county expelled the magistrate and held the walls.

sentences.zh: 諸將爭欲攻之，漢不聽，曰：『使鬲反者，皆守長罪也。

translations.literal: The generals vied to wish to attack them; Han did not listen and said: 'Those who made Ge rebel are all the fault of the magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: Every general wanted to storm it; Wu Han refused. 'The magistrate caused this revolt.'

sentences.zh: 敢輕冒進兵者斬。

translations.literal: Whoever dares lightly to rush advance with troops shall be beheaded.
translations.idiomatic: The first man to attack without orders dies.'

sentences.zh: 』乃移檄告郡，使收守長，而使人謝城中。

translations.literal: ' He then moved a proclamation telling the commandery to seize the magistrate and sent men to apologize within the city.
translations.idiomatic: He proclaimed the arrest of the magistrate and sent apologies into the town.'

sentences.zh: 五姓大喜，即相率歸降。

translations.literal: The five surnames were greatly pleased and immediately in succession came to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: The five clans rejoiced and submitted at once.

sentences.zh: 諸將乃服，曰：『不戰而下城，非眾所及也。』

translations.literal: The generals thereupon admired him and said: 'To take a city without fighting is not what the mass can reach.'
translations.idiomatic: The generals admitted, 'Taking a city without a blow—that is beyond us.'

sentences.zh: 冬，漢率建威大將軍耿弇、漢忠將軍王常等，擊富平、獲索二賊於平原。

translations.literal: In winter Han led grand general who establishes might Geng Yan, Hanzhong general Wang Chang, and others, and struck the Fuping and Huosuo two bandits at Pingyuan.
translations.idiomatic: That winter he led Geng Yan, Wang Chang, and others against the Fuping and Huosuo bands on the Pingyuan plain.

sentences.zh: 明年春，賊率五萬餘人夜攻漢營，軍中驚亂，漢堅臥不動，有頃乃定。

translations.literal: The next spring the bandits led more than fifty thousand men to attack Han's camp by night; in the army there was alarm and confusion; Han lay firm and did not move; after a time it settled.
translations.idiomatic: Next spring fifty thousand rebels raided his camp by night; panic spread until Wu Han lay still as stone until order returned.

sentences.zh: 即夜發精兵出營突擊，大破其眾。

translations.literal: That same night he sent crack troops out of camp to strike suddenly and greatly broke their host.
translations.idiomatic: He then sortied picked troops and broke them in the dark.

sentences.zh: 因追討餘黨，遂至無鹽，進擊勃海，皆平之。

translations.literal: He thereupon pursued and punished the remaining partisans, reaching Wuyan, advanced and struck Bohai, and all were pacified.
translations.idiomatic: He mopped up to Wuyan, struck Bohai, and cleared the region.

sentences.zh: 又從征董憲，圍朐城。

translations.literal: Again he followed in campaigning against Dong Xian and besieged Qu city.
translations.idiomatic: He joined the campaign against Dong Xian and besieged Qu.

sentences.zh: 明年春，拔朐，斬憲。

translations.literal: The next spring he took Qu and beheaded Xian.
translations.idiomatic: The next spring Qu fell and Dong Xian died on the block.

sentences.zh: 事已見《劉永傳》。

translations.literal: The affair is already seen in the 'Biography of Liu Yong.'
translations.idiomatic: The full account stands in Liu Yong's biography.

sentences.zh: 東方悉定，振旅還京師。

translations.literal: The east was wholly settled; he shook the troops and returned to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: The east pacified, he marched the army home to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 會隗囂畔，夏，復遣漢西屯長安。

translations.literal: It happened that Wei Ao rebelled; in summer again he dispatched Han to encamp west at Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: When Wei Ao rose that summer, the court sent Wu Han west to garrison Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 八年，從東駕上隴，遂圍隗囂於西城。

translations.literal: In the eighth year he followed the eastern imperial carriage up Long and thereupon besieged Wei Ao at Xicheng.
translations.idiomatic: In year 8 he accompanied the emperor onto Long and helped besiege Wei Ao at Xicheng.

sentences.zh: 帝敕漢曰：『諸郡甲卒但坐費糧食，若有逃亡，則沮敗眾心，宜悉罷之。

translations.literal: The emperor instructed Han, saying: 'The armored soldiers of the various commanderies only sit wasting grain; if there are those who flee, then they defeat the hearts of the host; it is fitting to dismiss them all.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor wrote, 'Idle garrisons only eat grain and desertions will break morale—disband the lot.'

sentences.zh: 』漢等貪並力攻囂，遂不能遣，糧食日少，吏士疲役，逃亡者多，及公孫述救至，漢遂退敗。

translations.literal: ' Han and the others greedily wished to join strength in attacking Ao and thereupon could not send them away; grain food daily grew less, officers and soldiers were weary in service, those who fled were many, and when Gongsun Shu's relief arrived, Han thereupon withdrew in defeat.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han wanted Ao crushed first and ignored the order; supplies shrank, men deserted, and when Shu reinforcements came he had to retreat in defeat.'

sentences.zh: 十一年春，率征南大將軍岑彭等伐公孫述。

translations.literal: In the spring of the eleventh year he led grand general on the south Cen Peng and others to punish Gongsun Shu.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of year 11 he marched with Cen Peng against Gongsun Shu.

sentences.zh: 及彭破荊門，長驅入江關，漢留夷陵，裝露橈船，將南陽兵及刑募士三萬人溯江而上。

translations.literal: When Peng broke Jingmen and drove straight into Jiang Pass, Han stayed at Yiling, fitted swift-oar boats exposed to view, and led Nanyang troops and three tens of thousands of amnestied convict soldiers up the river.
translations.idiomatic: After Cen Peng smashed Jingmen, Wu Han stayed at Yiling, rigged light craft, and took thirty thousand Nanyang men and pardoned convicts upriver.

sentences.zh: 會岑彭為刺客所殺，漢並將其軍。

translations.literal: It happened that Cen Peng was killed by an assassin; Han merged command of his army.
translations.idiomatic: When an assassin slew Cen Peng, Wu Han absorbed his command.

sentences.zh: 十二年春，與公孫述將魏黨、公孫永戰於魚涪津，大破之，遂圍武陽。

translations.literal: In the spring of the twelfth year he with Shu's generals Wei Dang and Gongsun Yong fought at Yufu Ford, greatly defeated them, and thereupon besieged Wuyang.
translations.idiomatic: In year 12 spring he routed Wei Dang and Gongsun Yong at Yufu Ford and closed on Wuyang.

sentences.zh: 述遣子婿史興將五千人救之。

translations.literal: Shu sent his son-in-law Shi Xing with five thousand men to rescue them.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Shu sent his son-in-law Shi Xing with five thousand to relieve the town.

sentences.zh: 漢迎擊興，盡殄其眾，因入犍為界。

translations.literal: Han met and struck Xing, utterly exterminated his host, and thereupon entered the borders of Qianwei.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han met Shi Xing, annihilated him, and crossed into Qianwei.

sentences.zh: 諸縣皆城守。

translations.literal: The various counties all defended their city walls.
translations.idiomatic: Every county barred its gates.

sentences.zh: 漢乃進軍攻廣都，拔之。

translations.literal: Han thereupon advanced the army to attack Guangdu and took it.
translations.idiomatic: He advanced on Guangdu and captured it.

sentences.zh: 遣輕騎燒成都市橋，武陽以東諸小城皆降。

translations.literal: He sent light cavalry to burn the market bridge of Chengdu; all the small cities east of Wuyang surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: His horsemen burned Chengdu's market bridge, and towns east of Wuyang yielded.

sentences.zh: 帝戒漢曰：『成都十餘萬眾，不可輕也。

translations.literal: The emperor warned Han, saying: 'Chengdu has a host of more than one hundred thousand; it cannot be treated lightly.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor cautioned him: 'Chengdu holds over a hundred thousand men—do not despise them.'

sentences.zh: 但堅據廣都，待其來攻，勿與爭鋒。

translations.literal: Only hold firm at Guangdu and await them to come attack; do not contend for the edge.
translations.idiomatic: Hold Guangdu and let them attack you; do not meet them head-on.'

sentences.zh: 若不敢來，公轉營迫之，須其力廢，乃可擊也。

translations.literal: If they do not dare come, you shift camp to press them; wait until their strength is exhausted, then you may strike.
translations.idiomatic: If they stay put, edge your camp forward until they weaken, then strike.'

sentences.zh: 』漢乘利，遂自將步騎二萬餘人進逼成都，去城十餘里，阻江北為營，作浮橋，使副將武威將軍劉尚將萬餘人屯於江南，相去二十餘里。

translations.literal: ' Han, riding success, thereupon himself led more than twenty thousand infantry and cavalry to press Chengdu, stopped more than ten li from the city, blocked the north of the river to make a camp, built a floating bridge, and ordered lieutenant general Wuwei general Liu Shang to lead more than ten thousand men to encamp south of the river, more than twenty li apart.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han, elated with victory, pushed twenty thousand men within ten li of Chengdu, camped north of the river with a pontoon, and parked Liu Shang with ten thousand south of the stream twenty li away.'

sentences.zh: 帝聞大驚，讓漢曰：『比敕公千條萬端，何意臨事勃亂！

translations.literal: The emperor heard and was greatly alarmed; he reproached Han, saying: 'Recently I instructed you in a thousand and ten thousand ways—how could you face the affair in sudden confusion!
translations.idiomatic: The emperor thundered, 'I warned you a thousand times—how could you blunder so at the crisis?'

sentences.zh: 既輕敵深入，又與尚別營，事有緩急，不復相及。

translations.literal: You have both despised the enemy and gone deep, and again encamped apart from Shang; if matters are urgent or slack, you can no longer reach each other.
translations.idiomatic: You drove deep in contempt of the foe and split from Liu Shang—neither can help the other now.'

sentences.zh: 賊若出兵綴公，以大眾攻尚，尚破，公即敗矣。

translations.literal: If the bandits send troops to tie you down and with the great host attack Shang, when Shang is broken you immediately are defeated.
translations.idiomatic: If they pin you while their main force swallows Liu Shang, you fall the moment he falls.'

sentences.zh: 幸無它者，急引兵還廣都。

translations.literal: If fortunately there is nothing else, urgently lead troops back to Guangdu.
translations.idiomatic: If disaster has not struck yet, pull back to Guangdu at once.'

sentences.zh: 』詔書未到，述果使其將謝豐、袁吉將眾十許萬，分為二十餘營，並出攻漢。

translations.literal: ' Before the edict arrived, Shu indeed sent his generals Xie Feng and Yuan Ji to lead a host of about one hundred thousand, divided into more than twenty camps, and all came out to attack Han.
translations.idiomatic: Before the order reached him, Gongsun Shu sent Xie Feng and Yuan Ji with some hundred thousand men in twenty camps against Wu Han.'

sentences.zh: 使別將將萬餘人劫劉尚，令不得相救。

translations.literal: He had another general lead more than ten thousand men to raid Liu Shang and make it impossible for them to rescue each other.
translations.idiomatic: A second column pinned Liu Shang so the two camps could not unite.

sentences.zh: 漢與大戰一日，兵敗，走入壁，豐因圍之。

translations.literal: Han fought a great battle one day, the troops were defeated, he ran into the ramparts, and Feng thereupon surrounded him.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han fought all day, broke, fled into his walls, and Xie Feng ringed the camp.

sentences.zh: 漢乃召諸將厲之曰：『吾共諸君逾越險阻，轉戰千里，所在斬獲，遂深入敵地，至其城下。

translations.literal: Han thereupon summoned the generals and spurred them, saying: 'We together, gentlemen, have crossed perils and obstructions, turned battle a thousand li, wherever we went beheading and capturing, and thus have gone deep into enemy land to reach below their walls.
translations.idiomatic: He rallied his officers: 'We have marched a thousand li through hell to stand under their walls.'

sentences.zh: 而今與劉尚二處受圍，勢既不接，其禍難量。

translations.literal: Yet now with Liu Shang we are surrounded in two places; the situation does not join; the disaster is hard to measure.
translations.idiomatic: Now Liu Shang and I are besieged apart with no link between us—disaster looms.'

sentences.zh: 欲潛師就尚於江南，並兵禦之。

translations.literal: I wish secretly to move the army to join Shang south of the river and combine troops to resist them.
translations.idiomatic: I mean to slip the army south to merge with Liu Shang.'

sentences.zh: 若能同心一力，人自為戰，大功可立；

translations.literal: If we can be of one heart and one strength, each man fighting for himself, great merit can be established;
translations.idiomatic: If we fight as one, each man for himself, we can still win.

sentences.zh: 如其不然，敗必無餘。

translations.literal: If it is not so, defeat will leave nothing over.
translations.idiomatic: If not, we are finished.

sentences.zh: 成敗之機，在此一舉。

translations.literal: The pivot of success and defeat lies in this one stroke.
translations.idiomatic: Everything hangs on this move.'

sentences.zh: 』諸將皆曰『諾』。

translations.literal: ' The generals all said 'Yes.'
translations.idiomatic: The generals shouted assent.'

sentences.zh: 於是饗士秣馬，閉門三日不出，乃多樹幡旗，使煙火不絕，夜銜枚引兵與劉尚合軍。

translations.literal: Thereupon he feasted the soldiers, fed the horses, closed the gates three days without going out, then raised many banners and flags and made smoke and fire unceasing; by night with bits in mouths he led troops to join armies with Liu Shang.
translations.idiomatic: He feasted the men, fed the horses, sealed the camp three days, lit fires and flags to mask his move, then marched by night with bits on the horses to join Liu Shang.

sentences.zh: 豐等不覺，明日，乃分兵拒江北，自將攻江南。

translations.literal: Feng and the others did not notice; the next day they then divided troops to hold the north of the river and himself led an attack on the south.
translations.idiomatic: Xie Feng noticed nothing until dawn, when he split his force north and led the rest south.

sentences.zh: 漢悉兵迎戰，自旦至晡，遂大破之，斬謝豐、袁吉，獲甲首五千餘級。

translations.literal: Han with his whole force met them in battle from dawn to late afternoon, thereupon greatly defeated them, beheaded Xie Feng and Yuan Ji, and took more than five thousand armored heads.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han turned with full strength, fought from dawn to dusk, slew Xie Feng and Yuan Ji, and counted five thousand heads.

sentences.zh: 於是引還廣都，留劉尚拒述，具以狀上，而深自譴責。

translations.literal: Thereupon he led back to Guangdu, left Liu Shang to resist Shu, reported the whole situation upward, and deeply blamed himself.
translations.idiomatic: He withdrew to Guangdu, left Liu Shang to face Shu, wrote a full report, and blamed himself harshly.

sentences.zh: 帝報曰：『公還廣都，甚得其宜，述必不敢略尚而擊公也。

translations.literal: The emperor replied, saying: 'Your returning to Guangdu was very fitting; Shu is sure not to dare slight Shang and strike you.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor answered, 'Your return to Guangdu was right—Shu will not ignore Liu Shang to hit you alone.'

sentences.zh: 若先攻尚，公從廣都五十里悉步騎赴之，適當值其危困，破之必矣。

translations.literal: If he first attacks Shang, you from Guangdu with fifty li of infantry and cavalry all hasten to him—you will meet him exactly in peril and distress, and breaking him is sure.
translations.idiomatic: If he strikes Liu Shang first, rush every rider and footman from Guangdu those fifty li and you will catch him exhausted and break him for certain.'

sentences.zh: 』自是漢與述戰於廣都、成都之間，八戰八克，遂軍於其郭中。

translations.literal: ' From this Han with Shu fought between Guangdu and Chengdu, eight battles eight victories, and thereupon encamped within their outer wall.
translations.idiomatic: After that Wu Han fought eight winning battles between Guangdu and Chengdu and camped under the outer walls.'

sentences.zh: 述自將數萬人出城大戰，漢使護軍高午、唐邯將數萬銳卒擊之。

translations.literal: Shu himself led several tens of thousands out of the city in a great battle; Han had protector of the army Gao Wu and Tang Han lead several tens of thousands of crack troops to strike them.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Shu sortied with tens of thousands; Wu Han sent Gao Wu and Tang Han with picked troops to meet him.

sentences.zh: 述兵敗走，高午奔陳刺述，殺之。

translations.literal: Shu's troops were defeated and fled; Gao Wu galloped into the formation and stabbed Shu, killing him.
translations.idiomatic: Shu's line broke; Gao Wu spurred into the ranks and speared Gongsun Shu dead.

sentences.zh: 事已見《述傳》。

translations.literal: The affair is already seen in the 'Biography of Shu.'
translations.idiomatic: The tale is told in Gongsun Shu's biography.

sentences.zh: 旦日城降，斬述首傳送洛陽。

translations.literal: At dawn the next day the city surrendered; they beheaded Shu's head and sent it in relay to Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn the city opened; Shu's head went by post to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 明年正月，漢振旅浮江而下。

translations.literal: The next year's first month Han shook the troops and floated down the river.
translations.idiomatic: The next first month he led the army downriver by boat.

sentences.zh: 至宛，詔令過家上冢，賜谷二萬斛。

translations.literal: When he reached Wan, an edict ordered him to pass his home and tend the ancestral tombs and bestowed twenty thousand hu of grain.
translations.idiomatic: At Wan the emperor let him visit kin and tombs and gave him twenty thousand hu of grain.

sentences.zh: 十五年，復率揚武將軍馬成、捕虜將軍馬武北擊匈奴，徙雁門、代郡、上谷吏人六萬餘口，置居庸、常山關以東。

translations.literal: In the fifteenth year again he led general who displays martial might Ma Cheng and general who captures the barbarian Ma Wu north to strike the Xiongnu, moved more than sixty thousand mouths of clerks and people of Yanmen, Dai, and Shanggu, and settled them east of Juyong and Changshan Pass.
translations.idiomatic: In year 15 he marched with Ma Cheng and Ma Wu against the Xiongnu and resettled sixty thousand people from Yanmen, Dai, and Shanggu east of Juyong and Changshan passes.

sentences.zh: 十八年，蜀郡守將史歆反於成都，自稱大司馬，攻太守張穆，穆逾城走廣都，歆遂移檄郡縣，而宕渠楊偉、朐□忍徐容等，起兵各數千人以應之。

translations.literal: In the eighteenth year the defending general of Shu commandery Shi Xin rebelled at Chengdu, styled himself grand marshal, attacked Governor Zhang Mu; Mu climbed over the wall and fled to Guangdu; Xin thereupon moved proclamations to commanderies and counties, while Danqu Yang Wei, Queren Xu Rong, and others raised troops, each several thousand men, to respond to him.
translations.idiomatic: In year 18 Shi Xin, garrison commander at Chengdu, rebelled as grand marshal, drove Governor Zhang Mu to Guangdu, called the commanderies to revolt, and drew Yang Wei of Danqu and Xu Rong of Queren with thousands each.

sentences.zh: 帝以歆昔為岑彭護軍，曉習兵事，故遣漢率劉尚及太中大夫臧宮將萬餘人討之。

translations.literal: The emperor because Xin formerly had been protector of the army for Cen Peng and was practiced in military affairs, for that reason dispatched Han leading Liu Shang and grand counsellor of the palace Zang Gong with more than ten thousand men to punish him.
translations.idiomatic: Knowing Shi Xin had served under Cen Peng and knew war, the emperor sent Wu Han with Liu Shang and Zang Gong and over ten thousand men.

sentences.zh: 漢入武都，乃發廣漢、巴、蜀三郡兵圍成都，百餘日城破，誅歆等。

translations.literal: Han entered Wudu, thereupon raised troops of Guanghan, Ba, and Shu three commanderies to besiege Chengdu; after more than a hundred days the city was broken and Xin and the others were executed.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han entered Wudu, levied Guanghan, Ba, and Shu, stormed Chengdu for a hundred days, took it, and executed Shi Xin.

sentences.zh: 漢乃乘桴沿江下巴郡，楊偉、徐容等惶恐解散，漢誅其渠帥二百餘人，徙其黨與數百家於南郡、長沙而還。

translations.literal: Han thereupon rode rafts down the river to Ba commandery; Yang Wei, Xu Rong, and others in fear and terror dispersed; Han executed more than two hundred of their chieftains, moved their associates several hundred households to Nan commandery and Changsha, and returned.
translations.idiomatic: He then boated down to Ba; Yang Wei and Xu Rong scattered in panic; he killed two hundred chiefs and deported hundreds of families to Nan and Changsha before returning.

sentences.zh: 漢性強力，每從征伐，帝未安，恒側足而言。

translations.literal: Han was by nature strong and forceful; whenever he followed in campaigning, while the emperor was not yet at ease he always stood on the sides of his feet and spoke.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han was iron-hard; on campaign he paced uneasily until the emperor felt secure.

sentences.zh: 諸將見戰陳不利，或多惶懼，失其常度。

translations.literal: When the generals saw the battle line go badly, many were frightened and lost their constant bearing.
translations.idiomatic: When the line wavered, other generals panicked.

sentences.zh: 漢意氣自若，方整厲器械，激揚士吏。

translations.literal: Han's spirit and bearing were as usual; he was just then arranging and sharpening weapons and gear, and encouraging officers and soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han stayed calm, sharpened weapons, and fired up the ranks.

sentences.zh: 帝時遣人觀大司馬何為，還言方修戰攻之具，乃嘆曰：『吳公差強人意，隱若一敵國矣！

translations.literal: The emperor at times sent men to observe what the grand marshal was doing; returning they said he was just then repairing tools for battle attack; he thereupon sighed, saying: 'Lord Wu is rather able to brace men's spirits—hidden like an enemy state!
translations.idiomatic: Spies found him repairing siege gear; the emperor sighed, 'Wu Han steadies the army like a rival power!'

sentences.zh: 』每當出師，朝受詔，夕即引道，初無辦嚴之日。

translations.literal: ' Whenever he went out on campaign, in the morning he received the edict, in the evening he immediately led the road; at first there were no days for preparing equipment.
translations.idiomatic: He could take orders at dawn and march at dusk with no 'readying day'.'

sentences.zh: 故能常任職，以功名終。

translations.literal: Therefore he could constantly hold office and end with merit and fame.
translations.idiomatic: That is how he kept high command and died covered in glory.

sentences.zh: 及在朝廷，斤斤謹質，形於體貌。

translations.literal: When he was at court he was scrupulously careful and plain, shown in body and bearing.
translations.idiomatic: At court he was modest and plain in every gesture.

sentences.zh: 漢嘗出征，妻子在後買田業。

translations.literal: Han once went on campaign while his wife and children behind bought fields and estates.
translations.idiomatic: Once while he was away his wife bought land.

sentences.zh: 漢還，讓之曰：『軍師在外，吏士不足，何多買田宅乎！

translations.literal: When Han returned he reproached them, saying: 'The army is outside; clerks and soldiers are insufficient—why buy so many fields and houses!
translations.idiomatic: He returned and scolded them: 'The army lacks pay—why hoard farms and houses?'

sentences.zh: 』遂盡以分與昆弟外家。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon he gave all to brothers and wife's kin.
translations.idiomatic: He gave the property away to kin and in-laws.'

sentences.zh: 二十年，漢病篤。

translations.literal: In the twentieth year Han was gravely ill.
translations.idiomatic: In year 20 Wu Han fell mortally ill.

sentences.zh: 車駕親臨，問所欲言。

translations.literal: The imperial carriage visited in person and asked what he wished to say.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor came to his bedside and asked his last word.

sentences.zh: 對曰：『臣愚無所知識，惟願陛下慎無亦攵而已。

translations.literal: He replied, saying: 'Your servant is foolish and has no knowledge; only I wish Your Majesty to be careful—nothing more than to grant pardons—that is all.
translations.idiomatic: He answered, 'I am a dull soldier; only beg you, Majesty, to be sparing with punishments and generous with pardons.'

sentences.zh: 』及薨，有詔悼湣，賜謚曰忠侯。

translations.literal: ' When he died there was an edict of mourning and pity and bestowed posthumous title Marquis Zhong.
translations.idiomatic: At his death the court mourned him as Marquis Zhong.'

sentences.zh: 發北軍五校、輕車、介士送葬，如大將軍霍光故事。

translations.literal: He raised the five colonels of the northern army, light chariots, and armored soldiers for the funeral, according to the precedent of grand general Huo Guang.
translations.idiomatic: His funeral matched Grand General Huo Guang's: northern-army colonels, light chariots, and armored escort.

sentences.zh: 子哀侯成嗣，為奴所殺。

translations.literal: The son Marquis of Ai Cheng inherited; he was killed by a slave.
translations.idiomatic: His son Cheng succeeded as Marquis of Ai but a slave murdered him.

sentences.zh: 二十八年，分漢封為三國：成子旦為灈陽侯，以奉漢嗣；

translations.literal: In the twenty-eighth year he divided Han's fief into three states: Cheng's son Dan was marquis of Quyang to continue Han's line;
translations.idiomatic: In year 28 the house was split: Cheng's son Dan took Quyang to maintain Wu Han's line;

sentences.zh: 旦弟盱為築陽侯；

translations.literal: Dan's younger brother Xu was marquis of Zhuyang;
translations.idiomatic: Dan's brother Xu received Zhuyang;

sentences.zh: 成弟國為新蔡侯。

translations.literal: Cheng's younger brother Guo was marquis of Xincai.
translations.idiomatic: Cheng's brother Guo took Xincai.

sentences.zh: 旦卒，無子，國除。

translations.literal: Dan died without a son and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Dan died childless and the fief lapsed.

sentences.zh: 建初八年，徙封盱為平春侯，以奉漢後。

translations.literal: In the eighth year of Jianchu Xu's fief was moved to marquis of Pingchun to continue Han's posterity.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 8 Xu was moved to Pingchun to carry the line.

sentences.zh: 盱卒，子勝嗣。

translations.literal: When Xu died his son Sheng inherited.
translations.idiomatic: Xu died; Sheng succeeded.

sentences.zh: 初，漢兄尉為將軍，從征戰死，封尉子彤為安陽侯。

translations.literal: At first Han's elder brother Wei was a general, followed in campaign and died in battle; Wei's son Tong was enfeoffed marquis of Anyang.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han's brother Wei had died in service; Wei's son Tong was enfeoffed at Anyang.

sentences.zh: 帝以漢功大，復封弟翕為褒親侯。

translations.literal: The emperor because Han's merit was great again enfeoffed younger brother Xi as marquis of Baoqin.
translations.idiomatic: For Wu Han's great merit the emperor also enfeoffed his brother Xi at Baoqin.

sentences.zh: 吳氏侯者凡五國。

translations.literal: The Wu clan held marquisates in all five states.
translations.idiomatic: Five branches of the Wu clan held marquisates.

sentences.zh: 初，漁陽都尉嚴宣與漢俱會光武於廣阿，光武以為偏將軍，封建信侯。

translations.literal: At first Yuyang commandant Yan Xuan with Han together met Guangwu at Guang'a; Guangwu made him lieutenant general and enfeoffed him marquis of Jianxin.
translations.idiomatic: Yuyang commandant Yan Xuan had joined Wu Han at Guang'a and became lieutenant general and marquis of Jianxin.

sentences.zh: 論曰：吳漢自建武世，常居上公之位，終始倚愛之親，諒由質簡而強力也。

translations.literal: The appraisal says: From the Jianwu era Wu Han constantly occupied the position of highest duke; from beginning to end he had the intimacy of leaning love—truly because he was plain in substance and strong in force.
translations.idiomatic: The historian says: Wu Han held top rank throughout Jianwu, always the emperor's favorite, for he was blunt, simple, and indomitable.

sentences.zh: 子曰『剛毅木訥近仁』，斯豈漢之方乎！

translations.literal: The Master said, 'Firm, resolute, dull, and slow of speech are near to humaneness'—is this not Han's type!
translations.idiomatic: Confucius called the firm and slow-spoken nearest to goodness—does that not fit Wu Han?'

sentences.zh: 昔陳平智有餘以見疑，周勃資樸忠而見信。

translations.literal: In antiquity Chen Ping's wisdom had surplus and therefore was suspected; Zhou Bo's substance was plain and loyal and therefore was trusted.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping's cleverness made him suspect; Zhou Bo's rustic loyalty won trust.'

sentences.zh: 夫仁義不足以相懷，則智者以有餘為疑，而樸者以不足取信矣。

translations.literal: When humaneness and righteousness are not enough to cherish one another, then the wise by surplus become suspect and the plain by insufficiency gain trust.
translations.idiomatic: When grace is thin, the clever man seems dangerous and the simple man seems safe.'

sentences.zh: 蓋延字巨卿，漁陽要陽人也。

translations.literal: Gai Yan, courtesy name Juqing, was a man of Yaoyang in Yuyang.
translations.idiomatic: Gai Yan, styled Juqing, came from Yaoyang in Yuyang.

sentences.zh: 身長八尺，彎弓三百斤。

translations.literal: His body was eight chi in height; he bent a bow of three hundred jin.
translations.idiomatic: He stood eight chi tall and drew a three-hundred-jin bow.

sentences.zh: 邊俗尚勇力，而延以氣聞，歷郡列掾、州從事，所在職辦。

translations.literal: Border custom esteemed courage and force, and Yan was famed for his spirit; he passed through commandery clerkships and staff posts in the province; wherever he was, duties were handled.
translations.idiomatic: The frontier prized strength; Gai Yan was famed for dash and excelled in every clerkship he held.

sentences.zh: 彭寵為太守，召延署營尉，行護軍。

translations.literal: When Peng Chong was governor he summoned Yan to act as camp commandant and acting protector of the army.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong made him camp commandant and acting protector of the army.

sentences.zh: 及王郎起，延與吳漢同謀歸光武。

translations.literal: When Wang Lang rose, Yan with Wu Han together plotted to return to Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Lang rose, he and Wu Han chose Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 延至廣阿，拜偏將軍，號建功侯，從平河北。

translations.literal: When Yan reached Guang'a he was appointed lieutenant general, titled marquis who establishes merit, and followed in pacifying north of the Yellow River.
translations.idiomatic: At Guang'a he became lieutenant general, marquis who establishes merit, and helped pacify Hebei.

sentences.zh: 光武即位，以延為虎牙將軍。

translations.literal: When Guangwu assumed the throne he made Yan general of the tiger fang.
translations.idiomatic: At the accession he was named tiger-fang general.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，更封安平侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu his fief was changed to marquis of Anping.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 his seat became the marquisate of Anping.

sentences.zh: 遣南擊敖倉，轉攻酸棗、封丘，皆拔。

translations.literal: He was sent south to strike Aocang, turned to attack Suanzao and Fengqiu, and took them all.
translations.idiomatic: He struck Aocang southward, then took Suanzao and Fengqiu.

sentences.zh: 其夏，督駙馬都尉馬武、騎都尉劉隆、護軍都尉馬成、偏將軍王霸等南伐劉永，先攻拔襄邑，進取麻鄉，遂圍永於睢陽。

translations.literal: That summer he supervised chief commandant of the consort's carriage Ma Wu, chief of cavalry Liu Long, protector commandant Ma Cheng, lieutenant general Wang Ba, and others in a southern campaign against Liu Yong, first attacked and took Xiangyi, advanced and took Maxiang, and thereupon besieged Yong at Suiyang.
translations.idiomatic: That summer he led Ma Wu, Liu Long, Ma Cheng, Wang Ba, and others south: Xiangyi and Maxiang fell, then he besieged Liu Yong at Suiyang.

sentences.zh: 數月，盡收野麥，夜梯其城入。

translations.literal: After several months he gathered all the wild wheat and by night scaled ladders into the city.
translations.idiomatic: After months he stripped the countryside of grain, then escaladed the walls by night.

sentences.zh: 永驚懼，引兵走出東門，延追擊，大破之。

translations.literal: Yong was alarmed and afraid, led troops and fled out the east gate; Yan pursued and struck and greatly defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yong fled out the east gate; Gai Yan pursued and routed him.

sentences.zh: 永棄軍走譙，延進攻，拔薛，斬其魯郡太守，而彭城、扶陽、杼秋、蕭皆降。

translations.literal: Yong abandoned the army and fled to Qiao; Yan advanced and attacked, took Xue, beheaded his Lu commandery governor, and Pengcheng, Fuyang, Zhuqiu, and Xiao all surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yong bolted to Qiao; Gai Yan took Xue, slew his governor of Lu, and Pengcheng, Fuyang, Zhuqiu, and Xiao submitted.

sentences.zh: 又破永沛郡太守，斬之。

translations.literal: Again he broke Liu Yong's Pei commandery governor and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: He next killed Liu Yong's governor of Pei.

sentences.zh: 永將蘇茂、佼彊、周建等三萬餘人救永，共攻延，延與戰於沛西，大破之。

translations.literal: Yong's generals Su Mao, Jiao Qiang, and Zhou Jian and others, more than thirty thousand men, came to rescue Yong; together they attacked Yan; Yan fought them west of Pei and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Su Mao, Jiao Qiang, and Zhou Jian brought thirty thousand to relieve Liu Yong; Gai Yan met them west of Pei and shattered them.

sentences.zh: 永軍亂，遁沒溺死者大半。

translations.literal: Yong's army was in disorder; those who drowned fleeing were more than half.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yong's army collapsed; more than half drowned in the rout.

sentences.zh: 永棄城走湖陵，蘇茂奔廣樂。

translations.literal: Yong abandoned the city and fled to Huling; Su Mao fled to Guangle.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yong fled to Huling while Su Mao ran to Guangle.

sentences.zh: 延遂定沛、楚、臨淮，修高祖廟，置嗇夫、祝宰、樂人。

translations.literal: Yan thereupon settled Pei, Chu, and Linhuai, repaired Gaozu's temple, and set up overseers, prayer masters, and musicians.
translations.idiomatic: He secured Pei, Chu, and Linhuai, restored Gaozu's temple, and appointed wardens, sacrificers, and musicians.

sentences.zh: 三年，睢陽復反城迎劉永，延復率諸將圍之百日，收其野穀。

translations.literal: In the third year Suiyang again turned the city in welcome to Liu Yong; Yan again led the various generals to besiege it a hundred days and gathered its wild grain.
translations.idiomatic: In year 3 Suiyang opened for Liu Yong again; Gai Yan besieged it another hundred days and stripped the fields.

sentences.zh: 永乏食，突走，延追擊，盡得輜重。

translations.literal: Yong lacked food, broke out and fled; Yan pursued and struck and wholly obtained the baggage train.
translations.idiomatic: Starving, Liu Yong broke out; Gai Yan chased him and seized the baggage.

sentences.zh: 永為其所殺，永弟防舉城降。

translations.literal: Yong was killed by his own men; Yong's younger brother Fang raised the city in surrender.
translations.idiomatic: His own men killed Liu Yong; his brother Fang surrendered the city.

sentences.zh: 四年春，延又擊蘇茂、周建於蘄，進與董憲戰留下，皆破之。

translations.literal: In the spring of the fourth year Yan again struck Su Mao and Zhou Jian at Qi, advanced and with Dong Xian fought at Liuxia, and broke them all.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth spring he beat Su Mao and Zhou Jian at Qi, then defeated Dong Xian at Liuxia.

sentences.zh: 因率平狄將軍龐萌攻西防，拔之。

translations.literal: Thereupon he led general who pacifies the Di Pang Meng to attack Xifang and took it.
translations.idiomatic: He then drove Pang Meng, general who pacifies the Di, against Xifang and captured it.

sentences.zh: 復追敗周建、蘇茂於彭城，茂、建亡奔董憲，董憲將賁休舉蘭陵城降。

translations.literal: Again he pursued and defeated Zhou Jian and Su Mao at Pengcheng; Mao and Jian fled in defeat to Dong Xian; Dong Xian's general Fen Xiu raised Lanting city and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: He routed Zhou Jian and Su Mao at Pengcheng; they fled to Dong Xian while Fen Xiu surrendered Lanting to the Han.

sentences.zh: 憲聞之，自郯圍休。

translations.literal: When Xian heard it, from Tan he besieged Fen Xiu.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Xian then left Tan to besiege Fen Xiu.

sentences.zh: 時，延及龐萌在楚，請往救之。

translations.literal: At that time Yan and Pang Meng were in Chu and requested to go rescue him.
translations.idiomatic: Gai Yan and Pang Meng were in Chu and begged leave to relieve him.

sentences.zh: 帝敕曰：『可直往搗郯，則蘭陵必自解。

translations.literal: The imperial edict said: 'You may go straight to strike Tan, then Lanting will certainly relieve itself.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor wrote, 'Hit Tan by surprise and the siege of Lanting lifts itself.'

sentences.zh: 』延等以賁休城危，遂先赴之。

translations.literal: ' Yan and the others because Fen Xiu's city was in peril thereupon first hastened to it.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing for Fen Xiu, they rushed to Lanting first.'

sentences.zh: 憲逆戰而陽敗，延等逐退，因拔圍入城。

translations.literal: Xian met them in battle and feigned defeat; Yan and the others pursued in retreat, thereupon raised the siege and entered the city.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Xian feigned flight; they broke the ring and entered the town.

sentences.zh: 明日，憲大出兵合圍，延等懼，遽出突走，因往攻郯。

translations.literal: The next day Xian greatly sent out troops in a combined siege; Yan and the others were afraid, hurriedly went out and broke through in flight, thereupon went to attack Tan.
translations.idiomatic: Next day Dong Xian closed the trap; they broke out and bolted for Tan.

sentences.zh: 帝讓之曰：『間欲先赴郯者，以其不意故耳。

translations.literal: The emperor reproached them, saying: 'The reason I wished you first to hasten to Tan was because of its unexpectedness.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor scolded them: 'I wanted Tan struck first for surprise.'

sentences.zh: 今既奔走，賊計已立，圍豈可解乎！

translations.literal: Now that you have already fled in disorder, the bandits' plan is set—how can the siege be relieved!
translations.idiomatic: You have lost the initiative—the enemy's plan is set and no siege lifts itself now!'

sentences.zh: 』延等至郯，果不能克，而董憲遂拔蘭陵，殺賁休。

translations.literal: ' When Yan and the others reached Tan, indeed they could not overcome it, while Dong Xian thereupon took Lanting and killed Fen Xiu.
translations.idiomatic: At Tan they stalled; Dong Xian stormed Lanting and slew Fen Xiu.'

sentences.zh: 延等往來要擊憲別將於彭城、郯、邳之間，戰或日數合，頗有克獲。

translations.literal: Yan and the others went back and forth intercepting and striking Xian's lieutenant generals between Pengcheng, Tan, and Pi; battles sometimes several engagements a day, and there were considerable captures.
translations.idiomatic: Gai Yan raided Dong Xian's columns between Pengcheng, Tan, and Pi, fighting daily with steady gains.

sentences.zh: 帝以延輕敵深入，數以書誡之。

translations.literal: The emperor because Yan despised the enemy and went deep several times used letters to warn him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor repeatedly wrote to curb his reckless advances.

sentences.zh: 及龐萌反，攻殺楚郡太守，引軍襲敗延，延走，北渡泗水，破舟楫，壞津梁，僅而得免。

translations.literal: When Pang Meng rebelled, attacked and killed the grand administrator of Chu commandery, led troops in a surprise strike and defeated Yan, Yan fled, crossed the Si River north, broke boats and bridges, and barely obtained escape.
translations.idiomatic: When Pang Meng murdered Chu's governor and ambushed Gai Yan, Yan fled north across the Si, burning boats and bridges to escape.

sentences.zh: 帝自將而東，征延與大司馬吳漢、漢忠將軍王常、前將軍王梁、捕虜將軍馬武、討虜將軍王霸等會任城，討龐萌於桃鄉，又並從征董憲於昌慮，皆破平之。

translations.literal: The emperor himself led east, summoned Yan with grand marshal Wu Han, Hanzhong general Wang Chang, former general Wang Liang, general who captures the barbarian Ma Wu, general who punishes the barbarian Wang Ba, and others to meet at Rencheng, punished Pang Meng at Taoxiang, and also together followed in campaigning against Dong Xian at Changlü, all broken and pacified.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor took the field east, joined Wu Han, Wang Chang, Wang Liang, Ma Wu, Wang Ba at Rencheng, crushed Pang Meng at Taoxiang, and with them destroyed Dong Xian at Changlü.

sentences.zh: 六年春，遣屯長安。

translations.literal: In the spring of the sixth year he was sent to encamp at Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth spring he was ordered to garrison Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 九年，隗囂死，延西擊街泉、略陽、清水諸屯聚，皆定。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, when Wei Ao died, Yan struck west Jiequan, Lueyang, Qingshui, and the various garrison settlements; all were settled.
translations.idiomatic: In year 9, after Wei Ao's death, he cleared the stockades at Jiequan, Lueyang, and Qingshui.

sentences.zh: 十一年，與中郎將來歙攻河池，未克，以病引還，拜為左馮翊，將軍如故。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year with general of the gentlemen of the palace Lai Xi he attacked Hechi; he did not take it; because of illness he led withdrawal and return; he was appointed left overseer of Fengyi, general as before.
translations.idiomatic: In year 11 he and Lai Xi besieged Hechi without success, fell ill, withdrew, and was named left overseer of Fengyi while keeping his general's rank.

sentences.zh: 十三年，增封定食萬戶。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year his fief was increased and fixed food income at ten thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: In year 13 his appanage was raised to ten thousand households.

sentences.zh: 十五年，薨於位。

translations.literal: In the fifteenth year he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in year 15.

sentences.zh: 子扶嗣。

translations.literal: The son Fu inherited.
translations.idiomatic: His son Gai Fu succeeded.

sentences.zh: 扶卒，子側嗣。

translations.literal: When Fu died his son Ce inherited.
translations.idiomatic: Gai Fu was followed by Gai Ce.

sentences.zh: 永平十三年，坐與舅王平謀反，伏誅，國除。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year of Yongping, because of plotting rebellion with his maternal uncle Wang Ping, he was executed and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongping 13 he was executed for conspiring with his uncle Wang Ping; the fief was stripped.

sentences.zh: 永初七年，鄧太后紹封延曾孫恢為蘆亭侯。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Yongchu Empress Dowager Deng continued the enfeoffment and made Yan's great-grandson Hui marquis of Luting.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 7 Empress Dowager Deng restored the line, enfeoffing Yan's great-grandson Hui at Luting.

sentences.zh: 恢卒，子遂嗣。

translations.literal: When Hui died his son Sui inherited.
translations.idiomatic: Hui died; Sui inherited.

sentences.zh: 陳俊字子昭，西陽西鄂人也。

translations.literal: Chen Jun, courtesy name Zizhao, was a man of Xie in Xiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Jun, styled Zizhao, came from Xie in Xiyang.

sentences.zh: 少為郡吏，更始立，以宗室劉嘉為太常將軍，俊為長史。

translations.literal: In youth he was a commandery clerk; when Gengshi was established, because imperial clansman Liu Jia was grand regular general, Jun was chief clerk.
translations.idiomatic: He was a county clerk; under Gengshi, Liu Jia as grand regular general made him chief clerk.

sentences.zh: 光武徇河北，嘉遣書薦俊，光武以為安集掾。

translations.literal: When Guangwu campaigned north of the Yellow River, Jia sent a letter recommending Jun; Guangwu made him clerk for gathering and settling.
translations.idiomatic: As Guangwu marched through Hebei, Liu Jia recommended him and he became clerk for pacification and recruitment.

sentences.zh: 從擊銅馬於清陽，進至蒲陽，拜強弩將軍。

translations.literal: He followed in striking the Bronze Horse at Qingyang, advanced to Puyang, and was appointed general of strong crossbows.
translations.idiomatic: He fought the Bronze Horse at Qingyang, advanced to Puyang, and was named general of strong crossbows.

sentences.zh: 與五校戰於安次，俊下馬，手接短兵，所向必破，追奔二十餘里，斬其渠帥而還。

translations.literal: He with the Five Schools fought at Anci; Jun dismounted, took short weapons in hand, wherever he turned was sure to be broken, pursued the rout more than twenty li, beheaded their chieftain, and returned.
translations.idiomatic: At Anci against the Five Schools he fought on foot with blade in hand, routed them twenty li, and took the chief's head.

sentences.zh: 光武望而嘆曰：『戰將盡如是，豈有憂哉！

translations.literal: Guangwu looked and sighed, saying: 'If battle generals were all like this, how could there be worry!
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu watched and cried, 'Were every captain like him, I would fear nothing!'

sentences.zh: 』五校引退入漁陽，所過虜掠。

translations.literal: ' The Five Schools led withdrawal into Yuyang, plundering wherever they passed.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Schools fled into Yuyang, looting as they went.'

sentences.zh: 俊言於光武曰：『宜令輕騎出賊前，使百姓各自堅壁，以絕其食，可不戰而殄也。

translations.literal: Jun spoke to Guangwu, saying: 'It is fitting to order light cavalry to go out before the bandits, make the hundred surnames each strengthen their walls, to cut off their food—without battle they can be exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Jun urged light horse to ride ahead of the rebels and seal every village so starvation would destroy them without a battle.'

sentences.zh: 』光武然之，遣俊將輕騎馳出賊前。

translations.literal: ' Guangwu approved it and dispatched Jun leading light cavalry to gallop out before the bandits.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu agreed and sent him ahead with cavalry.'

sentences.zh: 視人保壁堅完者，敕令固守；

translations.literal: Where he saw people whose protective walls were firm and complete, he ordered them to hold fast;
translations.idiomatic: Where hamlets were strong he ordered them to hold fast;

sentences.zh: 放散在野者，因掠取之。

translations.literal: those scattered in the open fields he thereupon plundered and seized.
translations.idiomatic: stragglers in the open he drove into his net.

sentences.zh: 賊至無所得，遂散敗。

translations.literal: When the bandits arrived they obtained nothing; thereupon they scattered in defeat.
translations.idiomatic: The rebels found no food and melted away.

sentences.zh: 及軍還，光武謂俊曰：『困此虜者，將軍策也。

translations.literal: When the army returned, Guangwu said to Jun: 'To trap these bandits was the general's stratagem.
translations.idiomatic: On the army's return Guangwu told him, 'That stratagem broke the enemy.'

sentences.zh: 』及即位，封俊為列侯。

translations.literal: ' When he assumed the throne he enfeoffed Jun as full marquis.
translations.idiomatic: At the accession Chen Jun received a full marquisate.'

sentences.zh: 建武二年春，攻匡賊，下四縣，更封新處侯。

translations.literal: In the spring of the second year of Jianwu he attacked the Kuang bandits, took four counties, and his fief was changed to marquis of Xinchu.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 spring he took four counties from the Kuang rebels and was moved to the marquisate of Xinchu.

sentences.zh: 引擊頓丘，降三城。

translations.literal: He led a strike at Dunqiu and subdued three cities.
translations.idiomatic: He stormed Dunqiu and brought three towns over.

sentences.zh: 其秋，大司馬吳漢承制拜俊為強弩大將軍，別擊金門、白馬賊於河內，皆破之。

translations.literal: That autumn grand marshal Wu Han acting by authority appointed Jun grand general of strong crossbows and separately struck the Jinmen and Baima bandits in Henei; all were broken.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn Wu Han named him grand general of strong crossbows and sent him against the Jinmen and Baima bands in Henei, which he crushed.

sentences.zh: 四年，轉徇汝陽及項，又拔南武陽。

translations.literal: In the fourth year he turned to campaign through Ruyang and Xiang and again took Nanwuyang.
translations.idiomatic: In year 4 he cleared Ruyang and Xiang and seized Nanwuyang.

sentences.zh: 是時，太山豪傑多擁眾與張步連兵，吳漢言於帝曰：『非陳俊莫能定此郡。

translations.literal: At that time the powerful men of Taishan for the most part held hosts and joined arms with Zhang Bu; Wu Han spoke to the emperor, saying: 'None but Chen Jun can settle this commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Taishan's magnates had joined Zhang Bu; Wu Han told the throne, 'Only Chen Jun can tame that commandery.'

sentences.zh: 』於是拜俊太山太守，行大將軍事。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon Jun was appointed grand administrator of Taishan, acting grand general affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Jun became governor of Taishan with general's powers.'

sentences.zh: 張步聞之，遣其將擊俊，戰於嬴下，俊大破之，追至濟南，收得印綬九十餘，稍攻下諸縣，遂定太山。

translations.literal: When Zhang Bu heard it he sent his general to strike Jun; they fought below Ying; Jun greatly defeated him, pursued to Jinan, collected more than ninety seals and cords, gradually attacked and took the various counties, and thereupon settled Taishan.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu sent a column; Chen Jun broke it below Ying, chased to Jinan, took ninety seals, and pacified Taishan county by county.

sentences.zh: 五年，與建威大將軍耿弇共破張步。

translations.literal: In the fifth year with grand general who establishes might Geng Yan he jointly broke Zhang Bu.
translations.idiomatic: In year 5 he and Geng Yan shattered Zhang Bu.

sentences.zh: 事在《弇傳》。

translations.literal: The affair is in the 'Biography of Yan.'
translations.idiomatic: The story is told in Geng Yan's biography.

sentences.zh: 時，瑯邪未平，乃徙俊為瑯邪太守，領將軍如故。

translations.literal: At that time Langye was not yet pacified; thereupon Jun was moved to be grand administrator of Langye, holding general as before.
translations.idiomatic: Langye still held out, so Chen Jun was shifted to governor there, keeping his command.

sentences.zh: 齊地素聞俊名，入界，盜賊皆解散。

translations.literal: The land of Qi had long heard Jun's name; when he entered the border, bandits and robbers all scattered.
translations.idiomatic: Qi had long heard his name; his border crossing alone scattered the brigands.

sentences.zh: 俊將兵擊董憲於贛榆，進破朐賊孫陽，平之。

translations.literal: Jun led troops to strike Dong Xian at Ganyu, advanced and broke the Qu bandit Sun Yang, and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: He struck Dong Xian at Ganyu, then crushed Sun Yang at Qu and pacified the region.

sentences.zh: 八年，張步畔，還瑯邪，俊追討，斬之。

translations.literal: In the eighth year Zhang Bu rebelled, returned to Langye; Jun pursued and punished him and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: In year 8 Zhang Bu revolted and fled back to Langye; Chen Jun ran him down and executed him.

sentences.zh: 帝美其功，詔俊得專征青、徐。

translations.literal: The emperor praised his merit and ordered Jun to be allowed independent campaigns in Qing and Xu.
translations.idiomatic: The court praised him and authorized independent operations in Qingzhou and Xuzhou.

sentences.zh: 俊撫貧弱，表有義，檢制軍吏，不得與郡縣相干，百姓歌之。

translations.literal: Jun cherished the poor and weak, displayed those with righteousness, checked and restrained army clerks so they could not interfere with commanderies and counties; the hundred surnames sang of him.
translations.idiomatic: He succored the weak, honored the worthy, kept soldiers from meddling in civil rule, and the people sang his praise.

sentences.zh: 數上書自請，願奮擊隴、蜀。

translations.literal: Several times he submitted memorials asking on his own, wishing to strike with vigor Long and Shu.
translations.idiomatic: He repeatedly begged to lead the assault on Long and Shu.

sentences.zh: 詔報曰：『東州新平，大將軍之功也。

translations.literal: The edict replied, saying: 'The east region is newly pacified—the grand marshal's merit.
translations.idiomatic: The rescript answered, 'The east is quiet thanks to the grand marshal.'

sentences.zh: 負海猾夏，盜賊之處，國家以為重憂，且勉鎮撫之。』

translations.literal: It backs on the sea and deceives the Xia; it is a place of bandits and robbers; the state takes this as heavy worry—for the moment exert yourself to guard and soothe it.'
translations.idiomatic: The coast still breeds rebels the court deeply fears—stay and hold it firm.'

sentences.zh: 十三年，增邑，定封祝阿侯。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year his fief was increased and fixed as marquis of Zhu'e.
translations.idiomatic: In year 13 his fief was enlarged and fixed at Zhu'e.

sentences.zh: 明年，征奉朝請。

translations.literal: The next year he was summoned for court audience duty.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he was recalled to court service.

sentences.zh: 二十三年卒。

translations.literal: In the twenty-third year he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died in year 23.

sentences.zh: 子浮嗣，徙封薪春侯。

translations.literal: The son Fu inherited; his fief was moved to marquis of Qichun.
translations.idiomatic: His son Chen Fu succeeded, later moved to Qichun.

sentences.zh: 浮卒，子專諸嗣。

translations.literal: When Fu died his son Zhuanzhu inherited.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Fu was succeeded by Chen Zhuanzhu.

sentences.zh: 專諸卒，子篤嗣。

translations.literal: When Zhuanzhu died his son Du inherited.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuanzhu was followed by Chen Du.

sentences.zh: 臧宮字君翁，潁川郟人也。

translations.literal: Zang Gong, courtesy name Junweng, was a man of Jia in Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: Zang Gong, styled Junweng, came from Jia in Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 少為縣亭長、遊僥，後率賓客入下江兵中為校尉，因從光武征戰，諸將多稱其勇。

translations.literal: In youth he was county village head and patrol scout; afterward he led retainers into the Lower Yangzi army as colonel and thereupon followed Guangwu in campaigns; the various generals often praised his courage.
translations.idiomatic: Youth as village head and scout; he then led clients into the Lower Yangzi host as colonel and followed Guangwu, famed for valor.

sentences.zh: 光武察宮勤力少言，甚親納之。

translations.literal: Guangwu observed that Gong was diligent in effort and spoke little, and was very intimate in accepting him.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu prized his silence and toil and kept him close.

sentences.zh: 及至河北，以為偏將軍，從破群賊，數陷陳卻敵。

translations.literal: When he reached north of the Yellow River he was made lieutenant general; he followed in breaking the mass of bandits and several times charged the line and drove back the enemy.
translations.idiomatic: In Hebei he became lieutenant general, broke bandit hosts, and repeatedly charged the enemy line.

sentences.zh: 光武即位，以為侍中、騎都尉。

translations.literal: When Guangwu assumed the throne he made him attendant at the palace and chief of cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: At the accession he was palace attendant and chief of cavalry.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，封成安侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu he was enfeoffed marquis of Cheng'an.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 he received the marquisate of Cheng'an.

sentences.zh: 明年，將突騎與征虜將軍祭遵擊更始將左防、韋顏於涅陽、酈，悉降之。

translations.literal: The next year he led shock cavalry with general who captures the barbarians Zhai Zun to strike Gengshi's generals Zuo Fang and Wei Yan at Nieyang and Li; all surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Next year he and Zhai Zun took Nieyang and Li from Zuo Fang and Wei Yan, who surrendered.

sentences.zh: 五年，將兵徇江夏，擊代鄉、鐘武、竹裏，皆下之。

translations.literal: In the fifth year he led troops to campaign through Jiangxia, struck Daixiang, Zhongwu, and Zhuli, and took them all.
translations.idiomatic: In year 5 he cleared Daixiang, Zhongwu, and Zhuli in Jiangxia.

sentences.zh: 帝使太中大夫持節拜宮為輔威將軍。

translations.literal: The emperor had a grand counsellor of the palace with staff of office appoint Gong general who aids might.
translations.idiomatic: A palace grandee with baton named him general who aids might.

sentences.zh: 七年，更封期思侯。

translations.literal: In the seventh year his fief was changed to marquis of Qisi.
translations.idiomatic: In year 7 his seat became Qisi.

sentences.zh: 擊梁郡、濟陰，皆平之。

translations.literal: He struck Liang commandery and Jiyin; all were pacified.
translations.idiomatic: He pacified Liang and Jiyin.

sentences.zh: 十一年，將兵至中盧，屯駱越。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year he led troops to Zhonglu and encamped at Luoyue.
translations.idiomatic: In year 11 he marched to Zhonglu and camped at Luoyue.

sentences.zh: 是時，公孫述將田戎、任滿與征南大將軍岑彭相距於荊門，彭等戰數不利，越人謀畔從蜀。

translations.literal: At that time Gongsun Shu's generals Tian Rong and Ren Man with grand general on the south Cen Peng opposed each other at Jingmen; Peng and the others several times fought without success; the Yue people plotted to rebel and follow Shu.
translations.idiomatic: Shu's Tian Rong and Ren Man faced Cen Peng at Jingmen with repeated stalemates while the Yue plotted to join Shu.

sentences.zh: 官兵少，力不能制。

translations.literal: Imperial troops were few and strength could not control them.
translations.idiomatic: Han troops were too few to hold them.

sentences.zh: 會屬縣送委輸車數百乘至，宮夜使鋸斷城門限，令車聲回轉出入至旦。

translations.literal: It happened that a dependent county sent several hundred supply carts arriving; Gong by night had them saw through the city gate thresholds and ordered the cart sounds to go in and out until dawn.
translations.idiomatic: When supply wagons arrived, he sawed through the gate sills and drove them clattering in and out until dawn.

sentences.zh: 越人候伺者聞車聲不絕，而門限斷，相告以漢兵大至。

translations.literal: Yue scouts who listened heard cart sounds unceasing and the thresholds broken; they told one another that Han troops had arrived in great force.
translations.idiomatic: Yue scouts heard endless wheels and broken sills and spread word that a great Han host had come.

sentences.zh: 其渠帥乃奉牛、酒以勞軍營。

translations.literal: Their chieftains thereupon presented oxen and wine to labor the army camp.
translations.idiomatic: The chiefs brought cattle and wine to the camp.
