sentences.zh: 宮陳兵大會，擊牛釃酒，饗賜慰納之，越人由是遂安。

translations.literal: Gong arrayed troops in a great assembly, struck oxen, strained wine, feasted, bestowed, and comforted and accepted them; the Yue people from this were thereupon settled.
translations.idiomatic: Zang Gong paraded his men, feasted the chiefs, and the Yue calmed down.

sentences.zh: 宮與岑彭等破荊門，別至垂鵲山，通道出秭歸，至江州。

translations.literal: Gong with Cen Peng and the others broke Jingmen, separately reached Mount Chuique, opened a road out to Zigui, and reached Jiangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: With Cen Peng he took Jingmen, opened the road past Mount Chuique to Zigui, and reached Jiangzhou.

sentences.zh: 岑彭下巴郡，使宮將降卒五萬，從涪水上平曲。

translations.literal: Cen Peng descended Ba commandery and ordered Gong to lead fifty thousand surrendered soldiers along the Fu River to Pingqu.
translations.idiomatic: Cen Peng sent him up the Fu with fifty thousand surrendered men toward Pingqu.

sentences.zh: 公孫述將延岑盛兵於沈水，時宮眾多食少，轉輸不至，而降者皆欲散畔，郡邑復更保聚，觀望成敗。

translations.literal: Shu's general Yan Cen massed troops on the Shen River; at that time Gong's host was many but food was little, transport did not arrive, and the surrendered all wished to scatter and rebel; commanderies and cities again guarded themselves in groups, watching success or failure.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Cen blocked the Shen while Zang Gong's supplies failed and his surrendered troops wavered; towns watched to see who would win.

sentences.zh: 宮欲引還，恐為所反，會帝遣謁者將兵詣岑彭，有馬七百匹，宮矯制取以自益，晨夜進兵，多張旗幟，登山鼓噪，右步左騎，挾船而引，呼聲動山谷。

translations.literal: Gong wished to lead withdrawal but feared they would turn on him; it happened the emperor sent a herald with troops to Cen Peng—there were seven hundred horses; Gong forged orders and took them to augment himself, advanced troops morning and night, greatly raised banners, climbed hills beating drums and shouting, infantry on the right and cavalry on the left, hugging boats and hauling, cries shaking the valleys.
translations.idiomatic: He dared not retreat but forged an order to seize seven hundred horses from an imperial column, then marched day and night with noise, flags, ships, and hills of drumfire.

sentences.zh: 岑不意漢軍卒至，登山望之，大震恐。

translations.literal: Yan Cen did not expect Han troops to arrive suddenly; climbing a mountain to look, he was greatly shaken and afraid.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Cen, climbing to look, panicked at the sudden Han host.

sentences.zh: 宮因從擊，大破之。

translations.literal: Gong thereupon followed with the strike and greatly defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Zang Gong fell on him and routed his army.

sentences.zh: 斬首溺死者萬餘人，水為之濁流。

translations.literal: Those beheaded and drowned were more than ten thousand; the water for it became a turbid flow.
translations.idiomatic: Ten thousand died by blade or water; the river ran brown.

sentences.zh: 延岑奔成都，其眾悉降，盡獲其兵馬珍寶。

translations.literal: Yan Cen fled to Chengdu; his host all surrendered; they wholly obtained his horses, armor, and precious goods.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Cen fled to Chengdu; his men surrendered with horses, arms, and treasure.

sentences.zh: 自是乘勝追北，降者以十萬數。

translations.literal: From this riding victory he pursued north; those who surrendered numbered in the tens of thousands.
translations.idiomatic: He pressed the rout until tens of thousands yielded.

sentences.zh: 軍至平陽鄉，蜀將王元舉眾降。

translations.literal: When the army reached Pingyang Township, Shu's general Wang Yuan raised the host and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: At Pingyang township Wang Yuan brought his command over.

sentences.zh: 進拔綿竹，破涪城，斬公孫述弟恢，復攻拔繁、郫。

translations.literal: He advanced and took Mianzhu, broke Fucheng, beheaded Gongsun Shu's younger brother Hui, and again attacked and took Fan and Pi.
translations.idiomatic: He took Mianzhu, Fucheng, slew Gongsun Hui, and seized Fan and Pi.

sentences.zh: 前後收得節五，印綬千八百。

translations.literal: Before and after he collected tally staffs to the number of five and seal cords to the number of one thousand eight hundred.
translations.idiomatic: He captured five tally-staffs and eighteen hundred seals in all.

sentences.zh: 是時，大司馬吳漢亦乘勝進營逼成都。

translations.literal: At that time grand marshal Wu Han also riding success advanced camp and pressed Chengdu.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Marshal Wu Han was already closing on Chengdu.

sentences.zh: 宮連屠大城，兵馬旌旗甚盛，乃乘兵入小雒郭門，歷成都城下，至吳漢營，飲酒高會。

translations.literal: Gong in succession slaughtered great cities; troops, horses, banners, and flags were very grand; he then rode the troops into the small Luo outer gate, passed below the wall of Chengdu, reached Wu Han's camp, and drank wine in a high assembly.
translations.idiomatic: Zang Gong sacked major towns, then paraded a splendid host through the outer gate of Xiaoluo, under Chengdu's walls, to Wu Han's camp for a feast.

sentences.zh: 漢見之甚歡，謂宮曰：『將軍向者經虜城下，震揚威靈，風行電照。

translations.literal: Han seeing him was very pleased and said to Gong: 'General, you lately passed below the bandit city, shaking and displaying might and numen, wind moving and lightning shining.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han hailed him: 'You thundered under their walls like wind and lightning.'

sentences.zh: 然窮寇難量，還營願從它道矣。

translations.literal: Yet cornered bandits are hard to measure; returning to camp I wish you would take another road.
translations.idiomatic: Yet cornered beasts are dangerous—take another road back to camp.'

sentences.zh: 』宮不從，復路而歸，賊亦不敢近之。

translations.literal: ' Gong did not listen; he returned by the same road; the bandits also did not dare draw near him.
translations.idiomatic: Zang Gong ignored him and returned the same way; the enemy dared not close.

sentences.zh: 進軍咸門，與吳漢並滅公孫述。

translations.literal: He advanced the army to the Xian gate and with Wu Han jointly extinguished Gongsun Shu.
translations.idiomatic: He marched on the Xian gate and with Wu Han ended Gongsun Shu.

sentences.zh: 帝以蜀地新定，拜宮為廣漢太守。

translations.literal: The emperor because Shu territory was newly settled appointed Gong grand administrator of Guanghan.
translations.idiomatic: For the new pacification of Shu he was named governor of Guanghan.

sentences.zh: 十三年，增邑，更封酂侯。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year his fief was increased and his enfeoffment was changed to marquis of Zan.
translations.idiomatic: In year 13 his appanage grew and he was moved to the marquisate of Zan.

sentences.zh: 十五年，征還京師，以列侯奉朝請，定封朗陵侯。

translations.literal: In the fifteenth year he was summoned back to the capital; as full marquis he attended court audiences; his fief was fixed as marquis of Langling.
translations.idiomatic: In year 15 he was recalled to Luoyang as a full marquis at audience and settled at Langling.

sentences.zh: 十八年，拜太中大夫。

translations.literal: In the eighteenth year he was appointed grand counsellor of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: In year 18 he became a palace grandee.

sentences.zh: 十九年，妖巫維汜弟子單臣、傅鎮等，復妖言相聚，入原武城，劫吏人，自稱將軍。

translations.literal: In the nineteenth year the sorcerer Wei Si's disciples Shan Chen, Fu Zhen, and others again gathered by false teachings, entered Yuanwu city, seized clerks and people, and styled themselves generals.
translations.idiomatic: In year 19 disciples of the wizard Wei Si—Shan Chen, Fu Zhen, and others—used occult talk to seize Yuanwu and declare themselves generals.

sentences.zh: 於是遣宮將北軍及黎陽營數千人圍之。

translations.literal: Thereupon Gong was sent to lead the northern army and several thousand men of the Liyang camp to besiege them.
translations.idiomatic: Zang Gong was sent with the northern army and Liyang garrison to besiege the city.

sentences.zh: 賊谷食多，數攻不下，士卒死傷。

translations.literal: The bandits had much grain food; several attacks did not take it; soldiers died or were wounded.
translations.idiomatic: The rebels were well stocked; repeated assaults failed and cost casualties.

sentences.zh: 帝召公卿諸侯王問方略，皆曰『宜重其購賞』。

translations.literal: The emperor summoned dukes, ministers, and princes of the blood to ask strategy; all said 'It is fitting to increase the posted reward.'
translations.idiomatic: The court asked high officials and kings for counsel; all urged heavier bounties.'

sentences.zh: 時，顯宗為東海王，獨對曰：『妖巫相劫，勢無久立，其中必有悔欲亡者。

translations.literal: At that time Xianzong was prince of Donghai; alone he replied, saying: 'Sorcerers coercing one another cannot stand long; among them there must be those who regret and wish to flee.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Liu Zhuang of Donghai alone said, 'Cult-led mobs cannot last; some inside already want to flee.'

sentences.zh: 但外圍急，不得走耳。

translations.literal: Only the outer siege is tight and they cannot get out.
translations.idiomatic: The tight cordon only traps them.'

sentences.zh: 宜小挺緩，令得逃亡，逃亡則一亭長足以禽矣。

translations.literal: It is fitting slightly to relax and ease, order that they may flee in flight; when they flee then a single village head is enough to capture them.
translations.idiomatic: Loosen the ring and let them scatter—a single village chief can round them up.'

sentences.zh: 』帝然之，即敕宮徹圍緩賊，賊眾分散，遂斬臣、鎮等。

translations.literal: ' The emperor approved it; immediately he ordered Gong to loosen the siege and ease on the bandits; the bandit host scattered; thereupon Chen and Zhen and others were beheaded.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed; Zang Gong opened the siege, the rebels scattered, and Shan Chen and Fu Zhen were caught and executed.'

sentences.zh: 宮還，遷城門校尉，復轉左中郎將。

translations.literal: When Gong returned he was promoted colonel of the city gates, then transferred to left leader of the gentlemen of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: He came back as colonel of the gates, then left leader of the gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 擊武溪賊，至江陵，降之。

translations.literal: He struck the Wuxi bandits, reached Jiangling, and made them surrender.
translations.idiomatic: He campaigned against the Wuxi rebels to Jiangling and accepted their surrender.

sentences.zh: 宮以謹信質樸，故常見任用。

translations.literal: Because Gong was careful, trustworthy, plain, and simple, therefore he was constantly seen employed.
translations.idiomatic: Trusted for his caution and plain honesty, he was kept in steady employ.

sentences.zh: 後匈奴饑疫，自相分爭，帝以問宮，宮曰：『願得五千騎以立功。

translations.literal: Later the Xiongnu suffered famine and plague and quarreled among themselves; the emperor asked Gong about it; Gong said: 'I wish to obtain five thousand cavalry to establish merit.
translations.idiomatic: When plague and famine split the Xiongnu, the emperor asked his view; Zang Gong asked for five thousand horse to strike.'

sentences.zh: 』帝笑曰：『常勝之家，難與慮敵，吾方自思之。

translations.literal: ' The emperor laughed and said: 'The ever-victorious household—hard to plan the enemy with them; I am just now thinking it over myself.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor smiled, 'Veterans of a hundred wins are hard to plan with—I will think on it myself.'

sentences.zh: 』二十七年，宮乃與楊虛侯馬武上書曰：『匈奴貪利，無有禮信，窮則稽首，安則侵盜，緣邊被其毒痛，中國憂其抵突。

translations.literal: ' In the twenty-seventh year Gong then with Marquis of Yangxu Ma Wu submitted a memorial, saying: 'The Xiongnu covet gain, have neither ritual nor trustworthiness; in extremity they knock their heads, in peace they invade and rob; along the border they suffer their poison and pain, and the central states worry their ramming and thrusting.
translations.idiomatic: In year 27 he and Ma Wu of Yangxu wrote: 'The Xiongnu are greedy and faithless, kowtow when desperate and raid when safe; the frontier bleeds while the heartland fears their thrusts.'

sentences.zh: 虜今人畜疫死，旱蝗赤地，疫困之力，不當中國一郡。

translations.literal: The barbarians now have men and livestock dying of plague, drought locusts and bare red earth; with the strength of plague and distress they do not match one commandery of the central states.
translations.idiomatic: Their herds die of plague, drought, and locusts—they are weaker than a single interior commandery.'

sentences.zh: 萬里死命，縣在陛下。

translations.literal: Their fate of death ten thousand li away hangs on Your Majesty.
translations.idiomatic: Their fate a thousand leagues away rests in your hands.'

sentences.zh: 福不再來，時或易失，豈宜固守文德而墮武事乎？

translations.literal: Good fortune does not come twice; the moment may easily be lost—how can it be fitting to hold fast to civil virtue alone and let military affairs slip?
translations.idiomatic: Fortune strikes once; to cling to culture alone and drop the sword would waste the hour?

sentences.zh: 今命將臨塞，厚縣購賞，喻告高句驪、烏恒、鮮卑攻其左，發河西四郡、天水、隴西羌胡擊其右。

translations.literal: Now order generals to the passes with rich posted rewards, proclaim to Gaogouli, Wuhuan, and Xianbei to strike their left, raise the four commanderies west of the River and Tianshui and Longxi Qiang and Hu to strike their right.
translations.idiomatic: Post generals on the frontier with rich bounties, bid Gaogouli, Wuhuan, and Xianbei hit their left flank, and raise the four western commanderies plus Tianshui and Longxi tribes for their right.'

sentences.zh: 如此，北虜之滅，不過數年。

translations.literal: Thus the destruction of the northern barbarians will not exceed several years.
translations.idiomatic: So the north could fall within a few years.'

sentences.zh: 臣恐陛下仁恩不忍，謀臣狐疑，令萬世刻石之功不立於聖世。

translations.literal: Your servant fears Your Majesty's humane grace cannot bear it, planning ministers hesitate, and the merit of carving in stone for ten thousand generations is not established in a sage age.
translations.idiomatic: I fear your kindness and doubting ministers may leave no stone monument for this sage reign.'

sentences.zh: 』詔報曰：『《黃石公記》曰，「柔能制剛，弱能制強」。

translations.literal: The edict replied, saying: The Record of the Duke of Yellow Stone says, "The soft can control the hard, the weak can control the strong."
translations.idiomatic: The rescript quoted the Record of the Duke of Yellow Stone, saying, "The soft masters the hard; the weak masters the strong."

sentences.zh: 柔者德也，剛者賊也，弱者仁之助也，強者怨之歸也。

translations.literal: The soft is virtue; the hard is injury; the weak is the help of humaneness; the strong is the return of resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Softness is de; hardness is harm; weakness aids humaneness; strength draws resentment.'

sentences.zh: 故曰有德之君，以所樂樂人；

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: the lord with virtue takes what he delights in and delights the people;
translations.idiomatic: The virtuous ruler shares his joys with the people.

sentences.zh: 無德之君，以所樂樂身。

translations.literal: The lord without virtue takes what he delights in and delights his own person.
translations.idiomatic: The vicious ruler hoards pleasure for himself.

sentences.zh: 樂人者其樂長，樂身者不久而亡。

translations.literal: He who delights in others, his joy is long; he who delights in himself is not long before he perishes.
translations.idiomatic: Shared joy endures; selfish pleasure dies young.'

sentences.zh: 舍近謀遠者，勞而無功；

translations.literal: He who abandons the near and plans the far toils without achievement;
translations.idiomatic: Neglect the near for the far and you toil for nothing.

sentences.zh: 舍遠謀近者，逸而有終。

translations.literal: he who abandons the far and plans the near rests and has an end.
translations.idiomatic: Secure the near and you rest with success.'

sentences.zh: 逸政多忠臣，勞政多亂人。

translations.literal: Easy government has many loyal ministers; toilsome government has many disorderly men.
translations.idiomatic: Light rule breeds loyal ministers; harsh rule breeds rebels.'

sentences.zh: 故曰務廣地者荒，務廣德者強。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: he who strives to widen territory is wasteland; he who strives to widen virtue is strong.
translations.idiomatic: He who chases land ends in waste; he who chases virtue grows strong.'

sentences.zh: 有其有者安，貪人有者殘。

translations.literal: He who keeps what is his is at peace; he who covets what others have is cruel.
translations.idiomatic: Content with your own, you are safe; grasping others', you are cruel.'

sentences.zh: 殘滅之政，雖成必敗。

translations.literal: A government of cruelty and destruction, though it succeeds, is sure to fail.
translations.idiomatic: Cruel rule, even if it wins, is doomed to fall.'

sentences.zh: 今國無善政，災變不息，百姓驚惶，人不自保，而復欲遠事邊外乎？

translations.literal: Now the state has no good government; disasters and prodigies do not cease; the hundred surnames are frightened and alarmed; men cannot preserve themselves—yet again you wish to undertake distant affairs beyond the border?
translations.idiomatic: The realm still quakes with omens and the people cannot save themselves—would you march beyond the wall now?'

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「吾恐季孫之憂，不在顓臾。

translations.literal: Confucius said: "I fear the troubles of the Jisun are not with Zhuanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "I fear the Jisun's trouble is not Zhuanyu.'

sentences.zh: 」且北狄尚強，而屯田警備傳聞之事，恒多失實。

translations.literal: " Moreover the northern Di are still strong, while matters heard from garrison farming and alert preparations often miss the truth.
translations.idiomatic: The northern Di remain strong, and frontier rumors often lie.'

sentences.zh: 誠能舉天下之半以滅大寇，豈非至願；

translations.literal: If truly one could raise half the realm to destroy the great bandit—would that not be the utmost wish;
translations.idiomatic: Were half the empire enough to crush them, who would refuse?'

sentences.zh: 茍非其時，不如息人。

translations.literal: if it is not the right time, it is better to give the people rest.
translations.idiomatic: If the hour is wrong, better give the people peace.'

sentences.zh: 』自是諸將莫敢復言兵事者。

translations.literal: ' From this the generals did not dare again to speak of military affairs.
translations.idiomatic: After that no general dared press for foreign war.'

sentences.zh: 宮永平元年卒，謚曰湣侯。

translations.literal: Gong died in the first year of Yongping; posthumous title Marquis Min.
translations.idiomatic: Zang Gong died in Yongping 1, posthumously Marquis Min.

sentences.zh: 子信嗣。

translations.literal: The son Xin inherited.
translations.idiomatic: His son Zang Xin succeeded.

sentences.zh: 信卒，子震嗣。

translations.literal: When Xin died his son Zhen inherited.
translations.idiomatic: Xin was followed by Zhen.

sentences.zh: 震卒，子松嗣。

translations.literal: When Zhen died his son Song inherited.
translations.idiomatic: Zhen was followed by Song.

sentences.zh: 元初四年，與母別居，國除。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yuanchu he lived apart from his mother and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanchu 4 he left his mother's house and lost the fief.

sentences.zh: 永寧元年，鄧太后紹封松弟由為郎陵侯。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongning Empress Dowager Deng continued the enfeoffment and made Song's younger brother You marquis of Langling.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongning 1 Empress Dowager Deng restored the line with Song's brother You at Langling.

sentences.zh: 論曰：中興之業，誠艱難也。

translations.literal: The appraisal says: The enterprise of the restoration was truly difficult.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: restoring the Han was hard work.

sentences.zh: 然敵無秦、項之強，人資附漢之思，雖懷璽紆紱，跨陵州縣，殊名詭號，千隊為羣，尚未足以為比功上烈也。

translations.literal: Yet the foe had not the strength of Qin and Xiang; men bore the thought of attaching to Han; though they clasped seals and wore cords, spanned commanderies and counties, bore strange names and deceitful titles, and a thousand bands formed hosts—still it was not enough to match merit with the highest deeds.
translations.idiomatic: Yet no enemy matched Qin or Xiang Yu; the people leaned to Han; though warlords sealed themselves across the map, none matched the founders' stature.

sentences.zh: 至於山西既定，威臨天下，戎竭喪其精膽，羣帥賈其餘壯，斯誠雄心尚武之幾，先志玩兵之日。

translations.literal: As for when west of the mountains was already settled and might faced all under heaven, the Rong were exhausted and lost gall and marrow, and the mass of commanders traded on leftover valor—this was truly the moment of fierce hearts still cherishing arms, the days of former intent dallying with war.
translations.idiomatic: Once the west was firm and awe filled the realm, the barbarians were spent while generals still hungered for battle—that was the hour of naked ambition.

sentences.zh: 臧宮、馬武之徒，撫鳴劍而抵掌，志馳於伊吾之北矣。

translations.literal: Men such as Zang Gong and Ma Wu stroked the ringing sword and clapped palms; their ambition galloped north of the Yiwu.
translations.idiomatic: Zang Gong and Ma Wu clapped hands over their blades, minds already racing toward the northern desert.

sentences.zh: 光武審《黃石》，存包桑，閉玉門以謝西域之質，卑詞幣以禮匈奴之使，其意防蓋已弘深。

translations.literal: Guangwu examined the Yellow Stone classic, kept deep-rooted caution, closed the Jade Gate to refuse the hostages of the Western Regions, and used humble words and silk to treat the Xiongnu envoys—the intent of his guard was already vast and deep.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu studied the Yellow Stone classic, minded the mulberry's deep roots, shut the Jade Gate on the western hostages, and answered the Xiongnu with humble gifts—his restraint ran deep.

sentences.zh: 豈其顛沛平城之圍，忍傷黥王之陳乎？

translations.literal: Could it be that he had been overturned at the siege of Pingcheng, or endured the wound of the tattooed king at Chen?
translations.idiomatic: His restraint ran deeper than the memory of Pingcheng or the fate of the king of Huaiyin?

sentences.zh: 贊曰：吳公鷙強，實為龍驤。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Lord Wu fierce and strong—truly the dragon's rearing.
translations.idiomatic: The verse runs: Fierce Wu Han—dragon rearing his crest.

sentences.zh: 電掃群孽，風行巴、梁。

translations.literal: Lightning swept the nests of villains; wind ran through Ba and Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Lightning cleared the nests; his wind swept Ba and Liang.

sentences.zh: 虎牙猛力，功立睢陽。

translations.literal: The tiger fang's fierce strength—merit set at Suiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Tiger-fang Gai Yan—his fame set at Suiyang.

sentences.zh: 宮、俊休休，是亦鷹揚。

translations.literal: Gong and Jun serene—those too were eagles soaring.
translations.idiomatic: Zang Gong and Chen Jun—eagles on the wing.
